fields.title: Abduction
fields.description: A person who, with specified intent, takes away or detains a woman against her will commits the offence of abduction. The offence is committed where the accused intends to marry or have sexual intercourse with the woman, or to cause her to be married or to have sexual intercourse with any other person.
fields.elements: ["[\"Accused is a person\", \"Accused takes the woman away or detains her\", \"The taking away or detention is against her will\", \"At the time of the act the accused has one of the specified intents (see mental elements)\"]"]

fields.title: Abduction of girls under sixteen
fields.description: A person who unlawfully takes an unmarried girl under the age of sixteen years out of the custody or protection of her parent or other person having lawful care or charge of her and against the will of the parent or of that person commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person who does the taking\", \"Victim: an unmarried girl under the age of sixteen years\", \"Act: taking the girl out of the custody or protection of her parent or other person having lawful care or charge of her\", \"Circumstance: the taking is against the will of the parent or of that person\", \"Lawfulness: the taking is unlawful (without lawful authority or consent)\"]"]

fields.title: Absence from extramural employment
fields.description: Where a person who has received permission to work outside a prison under the Prisons Act fails to present himself at the appointed hour or absents himself from that work, he commits an offence unless he satisfies the court that he had a reasonable and lawful excuse. Liability turns on the failure to attend or absence, subject to the court being satisfied of a lawful excuse.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person who has received permission to work outside a prison pursuant to the Prisons Act\", \"The accused failed to present himself at the appointed hour for that work or absented himself from the work\", \"The accused does not satisfy the court that he had a reasonable and lawful excuse for the failure or absence\"]"]

fields.title: Abuse of office
fields.description: A person employed in the public service who, in abuse of the authority of his office, does or directs to be done any arbitrary act prejudicial to the rights of another commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person is employed in the public service\", \"Person does an arbitrary act or directs an arbitrary act to be done\", \"Act is done or directed in abuse of the authority of the person's office\", \"Act is prejudicial to the rights of another\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for three years (where the act done or directed is for purposes of gain).

fields.title: Abusive language or brawling likely to cause a breach of the peace
fields.description: A person who uses obscene, abusive or insulting language to any other person in such a manner as is likely to cause a breach of the peace, or who brawls or in any other manner creates a disturbance in such a manner as is likely to cause a breach of the peace, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person who engages in conduct described below\", \"Uses obscene, abusive or insulting language to any other person; or brawls or otherwise creates a disturbance\", \"The conduct is in such a manner as is likely to cause a breach of the peace\"]"]

fields.title: Accessory after fact to murder
fields.description: Creates an offence for a person who becomes an accessory after the fact to murder. A person convicted of this offence is liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person\", \"The accused becomes an accessory after the fact to murder\"]"]

fields.title: Accessory after the fact to an offence punishable with three or more years imprisonment
fields.description: A person who becomes an accessory after the fact to an offence that is punishable with imprisonment for three years or more commits an offence. Where no other punishment is provided, the offender is liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"becomes an accessory after the fact\", \"to an offence that is punishable with imprisonment for three years or more\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for seven years (where no other punishment is provided).

fields.title: Act of gross indecency between persons
fields.description: A person who, in public or private, commits, is party to the commission of, or procures or attempts to procure the commission by any person of any act of gross indecency with another person commits an offence. On conviction the offender is liable to a fine of not less than one hundred thousand shillings and not exceeding three hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a term of not less than one year and not exceeding five years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused commits an act of gross indecency with another person\", \"Or is party to the commission of an act of gross indecency with another person\", \"Or procures or attempts to procure any person to commit an act of gross indecency with another person\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine of not less than one hundred thousand shillings and not exceeding three hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for a term of not less than one year and not exceeding five years.

fields.title: Acts intended to cause grievous harm or prevent arrest
fields.description: A person who, with intent to maim, disfigure or disable any person, or to do some grievous harm to any person, or to resist or prevent the lawful arrest or detention of any person, unlawfully commits any of the specified acts (a)–(g) in this section. Each such act done with the stated intent is an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused acted with one of the specified intents: to maim, disfigure or disable; to do grievous harm; or to resist or prevent lawful arrest or detention\", \"The accused unlawfully committed one of the prohibited physical acts listed in paragraphs (a)\u2013(g)\", \"The conduct was directed at or affected any person\"]"]

fields.title: Adulteration of drugs
fields.description: A person who adulterates any drug or medical preparation in such a manner as to lessen its efficacy, change its operation, or make it noxious, intending that it shall be sold or used for medicinal purposes as if it had not been adulterated, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Adulteration (act) of any drug or medical preparation\", \"Adulteration is in a manner that lessens efficacy, changes the operation, or makes it noxious\", \"Intention that the adulterated article shall be sold or used for medicinal purposes as if it had not undergone adulteration\"]"]

fields.title: Adulteration of food or drink intended for sale
fields.description: A person who adulterates any article of food or drink so as to make it noxious as food or drink, intending to sell the article as food or drink and knowing it is likely that it will be sold as food or drink, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Adulterates any article of food or drink\", \"Does so so as to make the article noxious as food or drink\", \"Intends to sell the article as food or drink\", \"Knows it is likely that the article will be sold as food or drink\"]"]

fields.title: Advertisements for stolen property
fields.description: A person who publicly offers a reward for the return of any property which has been stolen or lost and in the offer uses words purporting that no questions will be asked or that the person producing such property will not be seized or molested; or who publicly offers to return to any person who may have bought or advanced money by way of loan upon any stolen or lost property the money so paid or advanced, or any other sum or reward for the return of the property; or who prints or publishes any such offer, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"a person (actor)\", \"publicity: the offer is made publicly or is printed/published\", \"the offer relates to the return of property which has been stolen or lost\", \"the content of the offer either (i) purports that no questions will be asked or that the person producing the property will not be seized or molested, or (ii) offers to return to any person who may have bought or advanced money by way of loan upon the stolen or lost property the money so paid or advanced, or any other sum or reward for the return of the property\"]"]

fields.title: Advertising or making known to assist circulation or traffic in obscene matter
fields.description: A person who advertises or makes known by any means whatsoever, with a view to assisting the circulation of, or traffic in, any obscene matter, that a person is engaged in the acts referred to in this section, or advertises or makes known how, or from whom, any such matters or things can be procured either directly or indirectly, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Advertises or makes known by any means whatsoever information\", \"Such advertisement or information is with a view to assisting circulation of, or traffic in, obscene matter\", \"Content may be that a person is engaged in acts referred to in this section or how/from whom obscene matter can be procured\"]"]
fields.penalty: Fine of two hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for two years.

fields.title: Affray
fields.description: A person who takes part in a fight in a public place commits an offence. The offender is liable to a fine not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for six months.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Takes part in a fight\", \"The fight occurs in a public place\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for six months.

fields.title: Aiding, abetting or being accessory to mutiny
fields.description: A person who aids, abets, or is accessory to any act of mutiny by any member of the Police Force, Prisons Service, National Service or any other law enforcement organ commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person aids, abets, or is accessory to an act\", \"The act is mutiny\", \"The mutiny is committed by a member of the Police Force, Prisons Service, National Service, or any other law enforcement organ\"]"]

fields.title: Aiding, abetting or compelling prostitution by controlling a prostitute for gain
fields.description: A woman who, for the purpose of gain, exercises control, direction or influence over the movements of a prostitute in such a manner as to show that she is aiding, abetting or compelling her prostitution with any person, or generally, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Offender is a woman\", \"Exercised control, direction or influence over the movements of a prostitute\", \"The manner of that control shows she is aiding, abetting or compelling the prostitute's prostitution\", \"Conduct was for the purpose of gain\"]"]

fields.title: Aiding an enemy prisoner of war to escape
fields.description: A person who knowingly and advisedly aids an enemy of the United Republic who is a prisoner of war — whether the prisoner is confined in a prison or elsewhere, or is at large on his parole — to escape from his prison or place of confinement, or (if at large on parole) to escape from Mainland Tanzania, commits an offence punishable by imprisonment for life.
fields.elements: ["[\"The defendant aided another person to escape\", \"The person aided was an enemy of the United Republic who was a prisoner of war\", \"The escape was from the person's prison or place of confinement, or (if on parole) from Mainland Tanzania\", \"The aiding was done knowingly and advisedly\"]"]

fields.title: Aiding prisoners to escape
fields.description: A person who (a) aids a prisoner in escaping or attempting to escape from lawful custody, or (b) conveys anything or causes anything to be conveyed into a prison with intent to facilitate the escape of a prisoner, commits an offence. The offender is liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Accused aids a prisoner in escaping or attempting to escape\", \"The escape or attempt is from lawful custody (prisoner under lawful custody)\", \"Alternatively, accused conveys or causes anything to be conveyed into a prison\", \"Where conveyance is charged, accused acts with intent to facilitate the escape of a prisoner\"]"]

fields.title: Aiding suicide
fields.description: A person who procures another to kill himself, counsels another to kill himself and thereby induces him to do so, or aids another in killing himself commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for life.
fields.elements: ["[\"There is a person (the accused) who engages in conduct directed at another person\", \"The conduct consists of procuring, counselling (and thereby inducing), or aiding the other person in killing himself\", \"The other person kills himself as a result (required for the counselling alternative and implied for procuring/aiding)\"]"]

fields.title: Armed robbery
fields.description: A person who steals any property and, at or immediately before or after the stealing, is armed with a dangerous or offensive weapon or instrument and uses or threatens to use violence to any person in order to obtain or retain the stolen property, commits the offence of armed robbery.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person steals property (the theft act)\", \"At or immediately before or after the stealing the person is armed with any dangerous or offensive weapon or instrument\", \"At or immediately before or after the stealing the person uses or threatens to use violence to any person\", \"The use or threat of violence is in order to obtain or retain the stolen property\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for a term of not less than thirty years with or without corporal punishment.

fields.title: Arson
fields.description: A person who wilfully and unlawfully sets fire to certain property (a building or structure, a vessel, or a mine or its workings, fittings or appliances) commits an offence. The provision applies whether the building or vessel is completed or not.
fields.elements: ["[\"There is a person who commits the act\", \"The person sets fire to property\", \"The property is either: a building or structure (completed or not), a vessel (completed or not), or a mine or the workings, fittings or appliances of a mine\"]"]

fields.title: Assaulting, resisting or obstructing a person executing process or making a lawful distress with intent to rescue property
fields.description: A person who assaults, resists or obstructs any person engaged in the lawful execution of process, or in making a lawful distress, with intent to rescue any property lawfully taken under that process of distress, commits an offence under this section.
fields.elements: ["[\"Assault, resistance, or obstruction of a person\", \"The person targeted is engaged in the lawful execution of process or in making a lawful distress\", \"The conduct is done with intent to rescue property lawfully taken under that process of distress\"]"]

fields.title: Assaulting, resisting or wilfully obstructing a police officer or person acting in aid of a police officer in the due execution of duty
fields.description: A person who assaults, resists or wilfully obstructs any police officer in the due execution of his duty, or any person acting in aid of the police officer, commits an offence under this section.
fields.elements: ["[\"Assault, resistance, or wilful obstruction\", \"Target is a police officer acting in the due execution of his duty or a person acting in aid of that officer\"]"]

fields.title: Assault in pursuance of an unlawful combination or conspiracy relating to wages, trade, business or manufacture
fields.description: A person who assaults any person in pursuance of any unlawful combination or conspiracy to raise the rate of wages, or respecting any trade, business or manufacture or any person concerned or employed therein, commits an offence under this section.
fields.elements: ["[\"Assault of any person\", \"Assault committed in pursuance of an unlawful combination or conspiracy concerning: raising the rate of wages, or respecting any trade, business or manufacture, or any person concerned or employed therein\"]"]

fields.title: Assault occasioning actual bodily harm
fields.description: A person who commits an assault that occasions actual bodily harm commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for five years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused commits an assault against another person\", \"The assault occasions actual bodily harm to the victim\", \"Causation: the assault is the cause of the actual bodily harm\"]"]

fields.title: Assault on person on account of an act done by that person in execution of a legal duty
fields.description: A person who assaults any person on account of any act done by that person in the execution of any duty imposed on him by law commits an offence under this section.
fields.elements: ["[\"Assault of any person\", \"Assault motivated by (on account of) an act done by the victim in the execution of a legal duty\"]"]

fields.title: Assaults on persons protecting wreck
fields.description: A person who assaults and strikes or wounds any magistrate, officer or other person lawfully authorised in or on account of the execution of his duty in or concerning the preservation of any vessel in distress, or of any vessel or goods or effects wrecked, stranded or cast on shore or lying under water, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person who commits an act of physical violence\", \"The act consists of assaulting and striking or wounding\", \"The victim is a magistrate, officer or other person lawfully authorised\", \"The victim is acting in, or on account of, the execution of his duty concerning the preservation of a vessel in distress, or concerning any vessel or goods or effects wrecked, stranded or cast on shore or lying under water\"]"]

fields.title: Assault witj intent to commit an offence or to resist or prevent lawful apprehension or detainment
fields.description: A person who assaults another with intent to commit an offence, or with intent to resist or prevent the lawful apprehension or detainment of himself or any other person for any offence, commits an offence under this section.
fields.elements: ["[\"Assault of another person\", \"The assault is carried out with either: (a) intent to commit an offence; or (b) intent to resist or prevent the lawful apprehension or detainment of the accused or any other person for any offence\"]"]

fields.title: Assault with intent to steal
fields.description: A person who assaults any other person with intent to steal anything commits an offence. The offender is liable to imprisonment for a term of not less than five years but not more than fourteen years, with corporal punishment.
fields.elements: ["[\"Assault on another person\", \"Actus reus: an assault (unlawful physical attack or threat)\", \"Object: target is any other person\", \"Resulting or concurrent intention to steal anything\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for a term of not less than five years but not more than fourteen years, with corporal punishment.

fields.title: Assembling for purpose of smuggling
fields.description: Any two or more persons who assemble together for the purpose of unshipping, carrying, or concealing goods that are subject to customs duty and liable to forfeiture under customs law commit an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Two or more persons assemble together\", \"The assembly is for the purpose of unshipping, carrying, or concealing goods\", \"The goods are subject to customs duty and liable to forfeiture under any law relating to the customs\"]"]

fields.title: Attempted armed robbery
fields.description: A person who, with intent to steal anything from another person, is armed with a dangerous or offensive weapon or is in the company of one or more persons and in the course thereof threatens, or attempts to threaten, to use actual violence against any person commits attempted armed robbery. On conviction the person is liable to imprisonment for a minimum period of fifteen years with or without corporal punishment.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person (actor)\", \"With intent to steal anything from another person\", \"Is armed with any dangerous or offensive weapon or instrument, or is in the company of one or more persons\", \"In the course of that conduct threatens, or attempts to threaten, to use actual violence against any person\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for a minimum period of fifteen years with or without corporal punishment.

fields.title: Attempted rape
fields.description: A person who, with the intent to procure prohibited sexual intercourse with any girl or woman, attempts to commit rape by manifesting that intention (for example by threats, intimidation, false representations or representing himself as the woman's husband) commits the offence of attempted rape. On conviction the offender is liable to imprisonment for life and, generally, to imprisonment for not less than thirty years (with or without corporal punishment), subject to the sentencing variations in subsection (3) and (4).
fields.elements: ["[\"An individual (person) acts\", \"The individual manifests an intention to commit rape\", \"The manifestation is done with intent to procure prohibited sexual intercourse with any girl or woman\", \"The manifestation takes the form of conduct described in subsection (2) (threats; applying intimidation as a person of authority or influence; making false representations to obtain consent; representing oneself as the victim's husband and putting her in a position leading to involuntary carnal knowledge)\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for life; and in any case liable to imprisonment for not less than thirty years, with or without corporal punishment.

fields.title: Attempted robbery
fields.description: A person who assaults another with intent to steal anything, and at, immediately before, or immediately after the assault uses or threatens to use actual violence to any person or property in order to obtain the thing intended to be stolen or to prevent or overcome resistance to its being stolen, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"An assault on another person\", \"Intent to steal any thing\", \"At, immediately before, or immediately after the assault, the defendant uses or threatens to use actual violence\", \"The violence is directed at any person or at property\", \"The use or threat of violence is for the purpose of obtaining the thing intended to be stolen or of preventing or overcoming resistance to its being stolen\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for a term of not less than seven years and not exceeding twenty years, with corporal punishment.

fields.title: Attempting suicide
fields.description: A person who attempts to kill himself commits an offence. The provision criminalises attempts by an individual to cause their own death.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor is a person\", \"Actor commits an attempt to kill himself (takes steps towards causing his own death)\"]"]

fields.title: Attempting to injure by explosive substance
fields.description: A person who unlawfully, and with intent to do any harm of another, puts any explosive substance in any place whatsoever commits an offence. The offender is liable to imprisonment for fourteen years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor is a person who performs the act\", \"Actor puts or places any explosive substance\", \"Placement is in any place whatsoever\", \"Action is unlawful\", \"Actor has intent to do any harm to another person\"]"]

fields.title: Attempting to set fire to crops, etc
fields.description: A person who (a) unlawfully attempts to set fire to any thing mentioned in section 321, or (b) wilfully and unlawfully sets fire to anything so situated that any such thing mentioned in section 321 is likely to catch fire from it, commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Either: an unlawful attempt to set fire to any thing mentioned in section 321; or: wilfully and unlawfully setting fire to an object\", \"The object set on fire (in the second alternative) is so situated that any thing mentioned in section 321 is likely to catch fire from it\", \"The act is done unlawfully (i.e. without lawful excuse or justification)\"]"]

fields.title: Attempts at extortion by threats
fields.description: A person who, with intent to extort or gain anything from any person, (a) accuses or threatens to accuse any person of an offence (or of offering/making a solicitation or threat as an inducement to commit an offence), (b) threatens that any person shall be accused by another of an offence (or similar act), or (c) knowing the contents of it causes another to receive a writing containing such an accusation or threat, commits an offence. If the accusation or threat concerns specified serious offences (subparagraphs (i)–(iv)) the offender is liable to 14 years' imprisonment; otherwise to 3 years' imprisonment.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Mental element: intent to extort or gain anything from any person\", \"Prohibited conduct (one or more): (a) accuses or threatens to accuse any person of committing an offence or of offering/making any solicitation or threat as an inducement to commit/permit an offence; (b) threatens that any person shall be accused by another of an offence or similar act; (c) knowing the contents, causes any person to receive a writing containing any such accusation or threat\", \"Target: any person (the person accused or threatened to be accused, or recipient of the writing)\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for fourteen years where the accusation or threat involves the matters in subparagraphs (i)–(iv); otherwise imprisonment for three years.

fields.title: Attempts to cast away vessels
fields.description: A person who attempts unlawfully to cast away or destroy a vessel, whether completed or not, or who attempts unlawfully to do any act tending to the immediate loss or destruction of a vessel in distress, commits an offence. The offender is liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"An act constituting an attempt to cast away or destroy a vessel\", \"Alternatively, an act constituting an attempt to do any act tending to the immediate loss or destruction of a vessel in distress\", \"The conduct may be uncompleted (completion not required)\"]"]

fields.title: Attempts to procure abortion
fields.description: A person who, with intent to procure the miscarriage of a woman, unlawfully administers to her or causes her to take any poison or other noxious thing, or uses any force or any other means, commits an offence. The provision applies whether the woman is or is not with child and is punishable by imprisonment for fourteen years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Intent to procure the miscarriage of a woman\", \"Unlawful act consisting of at least one of: administering to the woman any poison or other noxious thing; causing her to take any poison or other noxious thing; using any force of any kind on her; or using any other means whatsoever\"]"]

fields.title: Attempt to commit an offence
fields.description: Creates an offence where a person attempts to commit an offence. Unless a different punishment is provided for the attempted offence, the offender is liable to imprisonment for two years or a fine or to both.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Attempts to commit an offence (conduct constituting an attempt to perpetrate a substantive offence)\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for two years or a fine or to both.

fields.title: Attempt to commit incest (male with female close relative)
fields.description: A male person who attempts to commit an offence under section 158 (incest as described in subsection (1)) is guilty of an offence. The attempt provision criminalises attempts to engage in the prohibited sexual intercourse with the close female relatives specified in subsection (1).
fields.elements: ["[\"Offender is a male person\", \"Offender takes steps constituting an attempt to commit the offence described in section 158(1) (incest)\"]"]

fields.title: Attempt to commit unnatural offences
fields.description: A person who attempts to commit any of the offences specified under section 154 commits an offence. On conviction the offender is liable to imprisonment for a term of not less than twenty years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused engaged in conduct constituting an attempt\", \"The attempted conduct was directed toward committing an offence specified in section 154\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for a term of not less than twenty years.

fields.title: Attempt to destroy or damage property by explosives
fields.description: A person who, unlawfully and with intent to destroy or damage any property, puts any explosive substance in any place whatsoever commits an offence. The offender is liable to imprisonment for fourteen years.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person places or puts an explosive substance\", \"Placement is in any place whatsoever\", \"Act is unlawful\"]"]

fields.title: Attempt to murder
fields.description: A person who (a) attempts unlawfully to cause the death of another; or (b) with intent to unlawfully cause the death of another, does any act or omits to do any act which it is his duty to do, the act or omission being of such a nature as to be likely to endanger human life, commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for life.
fields.elements: ["[\"the accused is a person\", \"the accused either: (a) attempts unlawfully to cause the death of another, or (b) with intent to unlawfully cause the death of another, does an act or omits to do an act which it is his duty to do\", \"where (b) applies, the act or omission is of such a nature as to be likely to endanger human life\", \"the conduct is unlawful\"]"]

fields.title: Attempt to set fire to thing referred to in section 319
fields.description: A person who attempts unlawfully to set fire to anything referred to in section 319 commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"the accused is a person\", \"the accused attempts to set fire to a thing referred to in section 319\", \"the attempt is unlawful (not authorised or justified)\"]"]

fields.title: Attendance at unlawful meeting for training others
fields.description: A person who is present at any meeting or assembly of persons, held without the permission of the Minister responsible for home affairs, for the purpose of training or drilling any other person to the use of arms or the practice of military exercises, movements or evolutions commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Act: is present at a meeting or assembly\", \"Purpose of meeting: training or drilling any other person to the use of arms or practice of military exercises, movements or evolutions\", \"Condition: meeting or assembly held without the permission of the Minister responsible for home affairs\"]"]

fields.title: Begging or causing a child to beg in public
fields.description: A person who wanders or places himself in a public place to beg or gather alms, or who causes, procures or encourages a child to do so, shall be deemed an idle and disorderly person and liable to the prescribed penalty.
fields.elements: ["[\"The person wanders or places himself in a public place for the purpose of begging or gathering alms; or\", \"The person causes, procures or encourages a child to beg or gather alms\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a period not exceeding three months or to both.

fields.title: Being armed, etc. with intent to commit an offence
fields.description: A person who is found in any of the circumstances (a)–(f) listed—such as being armed with a dangerous or offensive weapon with intent to break or enter and commit an offence, being armed at night with such intent, possessing instruments of housebreaking at night without lawful excuse, possessing such instruments by day with intent to commit an offence, having face masked or otherwise disguised with intent to commit an offence, or being in any building day or night with intent to commit an offence—commits an offence under this section.
fields.elements: ["[\"The defendant is a person found in one of the circumstances described in the section (see alternatives)\", \"The defendant either (a) is armed with a dangerous or offensive weapon/instrument, (b) is armed at night, (c) has in possession by night any instrument of housebreaking without lawful excuse, (d) has in possession by day any instrument of housebreaking with intent to commit an offence, (e) has face masked/blackened or is otherwise disguised, or (f) is in any building day or night\", \"There is an intent to commit an offence in or by means of the breaking/entry or other described conduct (except paragraph (c) where unlawful possession at night is itself an offence unless lawful excuse is shown)\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for five years, or if the offender has been previously convicted of an offence relating to property, imprisonment for fourteen years.

fields.title: Being found in a common gaming house
fields.description: A person, other than a person mentioned in subsection (1), who is found in a common gaming house is deemed, unless the contrary is proved, to be there for the purpose of unlawful gaming and commits an offence. Penalties are prescribed for first and subsequent offences.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person (not one of the persons described in subsection (1))\", \"Found in a common gaming house\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine of fifty thousand shillings for the first offence; for each subsequent offence a fine of four hundred shillings or imprisonment for three months or both.

fields.title: Being trained or present to receive training at unlawful meeting
fields.description: A person who, at any meeting or assembly held without the permission of the Minister responsible for home affairs, is trained or drilled to the use of arms or the practice of military exercises, movements or evolutions, or who is present at any such meeting or assembly for the purpose of being so trained or drilled, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Act (alternative 1): is trained or drilled to the use of arms or the practice of military exercises, movements or evolutions at a meeting or assembly\", \"Act (alternative 2): is present at such a meeting or assembly for the purpose of being trained or drilled\", \"Condition: the meeting or assembly is held without the permission of the Minister responsible for home affairs\"]"]

fields.title: Breach of trust by public officer
fields.description: A person employed in the public service who, in the discharge of the duties of his office, commits any breach of trust affecting the public commits an offence. The provision applies whether the breach of trust would have been criminal or not if committed against a private person.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person is employed in the public service\", \"Act is done in the discharge of the duties of the person's office\", \"Person commits any breach of trust\", \"The breach of trust affects the public\"]"]

fields.title: Breaking into building and committing an offence
fields.description: Creates an offence where a person breaks and enters certain non-dwelling buildings (including buildings adjacent to a dwelling and buildings used as places of worship) and commits an offence inside, or, having committed such an offence in those buildings, breaks out. The offender is liable to imprisonment for ten years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Breaks and enters one of the specified buildings (school house, shop, warehouse, store, workshop, garage, office, counting house, a building adjacent to and occupied with a dwelling house but not part of it, or any building used as a place of worship)\", \"Commits an offence inside that building\", \"Alternatively, having committed an offence in such a building, breaks out of the building\"]"]

fields.title: Breaking into building with intent to commit an offence
fields.description: A person who breaks and enters a school house, shop, warehouse, store, workshop, garage, office or counting house, or a building adjacent to a dwelling and occupied with it but not part of it, or any building used as a place of worship, with intent to commit an offence therein, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"breaks and enters a building\", \"the building is one of the specified types (see alternatives)\", \"acts with intent to commit an offence in the building\"]"]

fields.title: Brothels
fields.description: A person who keeps a house, room, set of rooms or place of any kind for the purposes of prostitution commits an offence. The offence targets the keeping of premises used for prostitution.
fields.elements: ["[\"a person (the accused) keeps a premises\", \"the premises is a house, room, set of rooms or place of any kind\", \"the premises are kept for the purposes of prostitution\"]"]

fields.title: Burglary (offence under section 294 committed at night)
fields.description: Where an offence under section 294 is committed in the night, the offence is burglary; the offender is liable to imprisonment for twenty years.
fields.elements: ["[\"An offence falling within section 294 is committed\", \"The offence is committed in the night (night-time)\"]"]

fields.title: Buying or disposing of any person as slave
fields.description: A person who imports, exports, removes, buys, sells or disposes of any person as a slave, or accepts, receives or detains against his will any person as a slave, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person who performs one or more prohibited acts in relation to another person\", \"Prohibited acts: importing, exporting, removing, buying, selling or disposing of a person as a slave; or accepting, receiving or detaining a person as a slave\", \"The other person is treated or intended to be treated as a slave (i.e. 'as a slave')\", \"Where detention is alleged, it is against the person's will\"]"]

fields.title: Carrying on or participating in a business dealing with obscene matter
fields.description: A person who carries on or takes part in any business, whether public or private, concerned with obscene matter, or deals in, distributes, exhibits publicly, or makes a business of lending any such matters or things commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Carries on or takes part in any business (public or private) concerned with obscene matter\", \"Or deals in, distributes, publicly exhibits, or makes a business of lending obscene matter\"]"]
fields.penalty: Fine of two hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for two years.

fields.title: Casting away vessels
fields.description: A person who wilfully and unlawfully casts away or destroys any vessel (completed or not); or wilfully and unlawfully does any act tending to the immediate loss or destruction of a vessel in distress; or who, with intent to bring a vessel into danger, interferes with any light, beacon, buoy, mark or signal used for navigation or exhibits any false light or signal, commits an offence. Such a person is liable to imprisonment for fourteen years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person who does at least one of the prohibited acts set out in the section\", \"Wilfully and unlawfully casts away or destroys any vessel, whether completed or not\", \"Wilfully and unlawfully does any act which tends to the immediate loss or destruction of a vessel in distress\", \"With intent to bring a vessel into danger, interferes with any light, beacon, buoy, mark or signal used for purposes of navigation, or exhibits any false light or signal\"]"]

fields.title: Causing another to use adulterated drugs for medicinal purposes
fields.description: A person who, knowing any drug or medical preparation to have been adulterated in such a manner as to lessen its efficacy, to change its operation, or to render it noxious, causes it to be used for medicinal purposes by any person not knowing of the adulteration commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor is a person\", \"The article is a drug or medical preparation\", \"The drug/medical preparation has been adulterated (so as to lessen efficacy, change operation, or render it noxious)\", \"The person knows of the adulteration\", \"The person causes the drug/medical preparation to be used for medicinal purposes by another person who does not know of the adulteration\"]"]

fields.title: Causing damage, etc., to railway works
fields.description: A person who (a) wilfully damages, injures or obstructs property acquired for or belonging to railway works; (b) pulls up, removes, defaces, destroys or interferes with markers or materials belonging to railway works; (c) commits any nuisance or trespass on land, buildings or premises acquired for or belonging to railway works; or (d) wilfully molests, hinders or obstructs the officer in charge of any railway or his assistants or workmen in the execution of work in relation to the construction or maintenance of the railway, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"An act (one or more of the acts described in alternatives) was committed\", \"The act affected property, materials, markers, land, buildings or premises acquired for or belonging to railway works, or officers/assistants/workmen in charge of railway works\", \"The act falls within one of the prohibited categories (damage, injure, obstruct, pull up, remove, deface, destroy, interfere, commit nuisance or trespass, wilfully molest, hinder or obstruct)\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine of not less than five million shillings but not exceeding ten million shillings or to imprisonment for a term of not less than two years but not exceeding five years or to both.

fields.title: Causing disturbance to persons assembled for funeral ceremonies
fields.description: A person who, with the intention of wounding the feelings of any person or of insulting the religion of any person, or with the knowledge that such feelings or religion are likely to be insulted, causes disturbance to any persons assembled for the purpose of funeral ceremonies commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused caused a disturbance\", \"The disturbance was directed at persons assembled for the purpose of funeral ceremonies\", \"The accused acted with either the intention of wounding the feelings of any person or of insulting the religion of any person, or with knowledge that the feelings of any person are likely to be wounded or that the religion of any person is likely to be insulted thereby\"]"]

fields.title: Causing pecuniary loss or damage to property of specified authority
fields.description: A person who, whether or not an employee of a specified authority, by any willful act or omission or by negligence, misconduct or failure to take reasonable care or to discharge duties in a reasonable manner, causes a specified authority to suffer pecuniary loss or causes damage to property owned by or in the possession of a specified authority, where the loss or damage has a monetary value of not less than ten million shillings, commits an offence. On conviction the person is liable to a fine of not less than five million shillings or imprisonment for a term of not less than three years or both.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person (whether or not an employee of the specified authority)\", \"By a willful act or omission, or by negligence, misconduct, or failure to take reasonable care or to discharge duties in a reasonable manner\", \"Causes a specified authority to suffer pecuniary loss OR causes damage to property owned by or in the possession of a specified authority\", \"The pecuniary loss or damage has a monetary value of not less than ten million shillings\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine of not less than five million shillings or imprisonment for a term of not less than three years or to both.

fields.title: Challenge to fight duel
fields.description: A person who challenges another to fight a duel, attempts to provoke another to fight a duel, or attempts to provoke any person to challenge another to fight a duel commits an offence. The provision criminalises issuing a challenge or attempting to provoke a duel or a challenge to duel.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person (the actor) performs one of the prohibited acts\", \"Prohibited acts: (a) challenges another to fight a duel; (b) attempts to provoke another to fight a duel; (c) attempts to provoke any person to challenge another to fight a duel\"]"]

fields.title: Cheating
fields.description: A person who, by means of any fraudulent trick or device, obtains from another person property or causes another person to deliver or pay property (including money or goods), or obtains a greater sum or quantity than would have been paid or delivered but for the trick, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"By means of any fraudulent trick or device\", \"Obtains from any other person anything capable of being stolen, or\", \"Causes any other person to deliver to any person anything capable of being stolen, or\", \"Causes any other person to pay or deliver any money or goods, or\", \"Obtains any greater sum of money or greater quantity of goods than would have been paid or delivered but for the trick or device\"]"]

fields.title: Child destruction
fields.description: A person who, with intent to destroy the life of a child capable of being born alive, by any wilful act causes the child to die before it has an existence independent of its mother, is guilty of child destruction and liable to imprisonment for life.
fields.elements: ["[\"actor: a person\", \"victim: a child capable of being born alive (an unborn child)\", \"the child dies before having an existence independent of its mother\", \"a wilful act by the actor causes the death of the child\", \"the actor has intent to destroy the life of the child\"]"]

fields.title: Child stealing
fields.description: A person who, with intent to deprive a parent, guardian or other person who has the lawful care or charge of a child under the age of eighteen years of the possession of that child, forcibly or fraudulently takes or entices away or detains the child, or receives or harbours the child knowing it to have been so taken, enticed away or detained, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"an accused person (person)\", \"the accused acted with intent to deprive a parent, guardian or other person who has lawful care or charge of the child of possession of the child\", \"the child is under the age of eighteen years\", \"prohibited act: forcibly or fraudulently takes or entices away, or detains the child\", \"alternative prohibited act: receives or harbours the child knowing it to have been so taken, enticed away or detained\"]"]

fields.title: Clipping
fields.description: A person who deals with any coin in such a manner as to diminish its weight, with intent that when so dealt with it may pass as coin, commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person deals with any coin\", \"The dealing is done in such a manner as to diminish the coin's weight\", \"The person intends that, after being so dealt with, the coin may pass as coin\"]"]

fields.title: Common assault
fields.description: A person who unlawfully assaults another commits an offence. Where the assault is not committed in circumstances attracting a greater punishment elsewhere in this Code, the offender is liable to imprisonment for one year.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused assaulted another person\", \"The assault was unlawful\"]"]

fields.title: Common nuisance
fields.description: A person who does an act not authorised by law or omits to discharge a legal duty and thereby causes any common injury, danger or annoyance, or obstructs or causes inconvenience to the public in the exercise of common rights commits the offence of common nuisance. The person is liable to imprisonment for one year.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person (natural person)\", \"An act not authorised by law OR an omission to discharge a legal duty\", \"That act or omission causes any common injury, danger or annoyance OR obstructs or causes inconvenience to the public\", \"The injury/danger/annoyance or obstruction/inconvenience is to the public in the exercise of common rights\"]"]

fields.title: Communicating infectious diseases to animal
fields.description: A person who wilfully and unlawfully causes, is concerned in causing, or attempts to cause any infectious disease to be communicated to or among any animal or animals capable of being stolen commits an offence punishable by imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The actor is a person\", \"The actor wilfully and unlawfully causes, is concerned in causing, or attempts to cause\", \"An infectious disease is communicated to or among animals\", \"The animals are capable of being stolen\"]"]

fields.title: Compounding penal actions
fields.description: A person who, having brought or under pretence of bringing a criminal charge against another under any written law in order to obtain from that person a penalty for an offence (committed or alleged), compounds the criminal charge without the order or consent of the court in which the charge is or is to be brought commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person brought, or under pretence of bringing, a criminal charge against another person\", \"The charge is founded on any written law\", \"The charge was brought in order to obtain from that person a penalty for an offence (committed or alleged)\", \"The person compounds the criminal charge\", \"Compounding occurs without the order or consent of the court in which the charge is brought or is to be brought\"]"]

fields.title: Concealing birth of child
fields.description: A person who, when a woman is delivered of a child, endeavours by any secret disposition of the dead body of the child to conceal the birth, whether the child died before, at or after its birth, commits an offence. The provision criminalises efforts to hide the fact of the birth by secretly disposing of the child's dead body.
fields.elements: ["[\"The actor is a person (anyone)\", \"A woman has been delivered of a child (the relevant event has occurred)\", \"The child is dead (died before, at, or after birth) and there is a dead body\", \"The person endeavours to conceal the birth\", \"The endeavour is effected by any secret disposition of the dead body of the child\"]"]

fields.title: Concealing deeds
fields.description: A person who, with intent to defraud, conceals the whole or part of any document which is evidence of title to any land or estate in land commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for three years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Concealment (whole or part) of a document\", \"The document is evidence of title to any land or estate in land\"]"]

fields.title: Concealing registers
fields.description: A person who, with intent to defraud, conceals or takes from its place of deposit any register that is authorised or required by law to be kept for authenticating or recording title to property, or for recording births, baptisms, marriages, deaths or burials, or a copy of any part of a register required by law to be sent to any public office, commits an offence punishable by imprisonment for ten years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person\", \"The accused conceals or takes from its place of deposit\", \"The thing concealed or taken is any register (or a copy of any part of a register)\", \"The register is authorised or required by law to be kept for authenticating or recording the title to property, or for recording births, baptisms, marriages, deaths or burials, or the copy is required by law to be sent to a public office\", \"The act is done with intent to defraud\"]"]

fields.title: Concealing wills
fields.description: A person who, with intent to defraud, conceals any testamentary instrument, whether the testator is living or dead, commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for ten years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor is a person\", \"Actor conceals a testamentary instrument (will)\", \"Concealment is done with intent to defraud\"]"]

fields.title: Conducting or managing a pyramid scheme
fields.description: A person who conducts or manages a pyramid scheme commits an offence. A "pyramid scheme" is defined to include operations whereby a person (a) holds out a promise of receiving a sum or valuable security greater than the money or security given (including by payment or giving security or by obliging others to pay or give security), (b) makes payments to promoters or participants, or (c) sends or causes chain letters or sends or receives money or money's worth in connection with chain letters; the section also specifies indicia and applications of such schemes and certain exemptions.
fields.elements: ["[\"the accused is a person who conducts or manages\", \"the conduct consists of operating or managing a 'pyramid scheme' (as defined in subsection (2))\"]"]
fields.penalty: On conviction, liable to a fine not exceeding five million shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding five years (subsection (3)).

fields.title: Conspiracy to commit an offence punishable with imprisonment for less than three years
fields.description: A person who conspires with another to commit an offence punishable with imprisonment for a term of less than three years commits an offence. The provision also criminalises conspiring to do any act anywhere in the world which, if done in Tanzania, would be an offence so punishable and which is an offence under the laws of the place where it is proposed to be done.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person conspires (agrees) with another person\", \"The conspiracy is to commit a substantive offence punishable with imprisonment for a term of less than three years\", \"Alternatively, the conspiracy is to do any act anywhere in the world which, if done in Tanzania, would be an offence so punishable\", \"Where the act is abroad, the act must also be an offence under the laws in force in the place where it is proposed to be done\"]"]

fields.title: Conspiracy to commit an offence punishable with imprisonment for three or more years
fields.description: A person who conspires with another to commit any offence punishable with imprisonment for a term of three years or more, or to do any act in any part of the world which, if done in Tanzania, would be such an offence and which is an offence under the law of the place where it is proposed to be done, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person conspires (agrees) with another\", \"The object of the conspiracy is to commit an offence punishable with imprisonment for a term of three years or more\", \"Or the object is to do an act in any part of the world which, if done in Tanzania, would be such an offence and which is an offence under the laws of the place where it is proposed to be done\"]"]
fields.penalty: Where no other punishment is provided, to imprisonment for seven years or, where the greatest punishment to which a person convicted of the offence in question shall be liable is less than imprisonment for seven years, then to such lesser punishment.

fields.title: Conspiracy to defeat justice
fields.description: A person who conspires with any other person to accuse any person falsely of any crime or to do anything to obstruct, prevent, pervert, or defeat the course of justice commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for five years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Conspiracy: an agreement with any other person\", \"Object of the agreement: to accuse any person falsely of any crime\", \"OR object of the agreement: to do anything to obstruct, prevent, pervert, or defeat the course of justice\"]"]

fields.title: Conspiracy to defraud
fields.description: A person who conspires with another by deceit or by any fraudulent means with the purpose of affecting the market price of anything publicly sold, or of defrauding the public or any person, or of extorting property from any person, commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for five years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Conspired (agreement) with another person\", \"Conspiracy carried out by deceit or by any fraudulent means\", \"Conspiracy had the purpose of either: (a) affecting the market price of anything publicly sold, (b) defrauding the public or any person, or (c) extorting any property from any person\"]"]

fields.title: Conspiracy to induce unlawful sexual intercourse
fields.description: A person who conspires with another to induce any woman, by means of any false pretence or other fraudulent means, to permit a man to have unlawful sexual intercourse with her commits an offence. The offence carries liability to imprisonment for three years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Conspires (agrees) with another person\", \"Intends to induce any woman to permit a man to have unlawful sexual intercourse with her\", \"Employs means that are false pretences or other fraudulent means to induce her\"]"]

fields.title: Conspiracy to murder
fields.description: A person who conspires with any other person to kill any person, whether that person is in Mainland Tanzania or elsewhere, commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for fourteen years.
fields.elements: ["[\"An agreement or conspiracy with at least one other person\", \"The agreement is to kill (cause the death of) any person\"]"]

fields.title: Contempt of court
fields.description: A person who commits any of the acts listed in subsection (1)(a)–(k) in relation to a judicial proceeding or any person before whom the proceeding is being heard or taken commits an offence of contempt of court and is liable to a fine not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for six months.
fields.elements: ["[\"The actor is a person\", \"There is a judicial proceeding or a person before whom a judicial proceeding is being heard or taken\", \"The actor engages in one or more of the prohibited acts described in subsection (1)(a)\u2013(k) (see alternatives)\", \"When applicable, the act is wrongful, intentional or done without lawful excuse as specified in the text\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for six months.

fields.title: Conversion not amounting to theft
fields.description: A person who unlawfully and without colour of right, but not so as to be guilty of theft, takes or converts to his use or to the use of any other person any draught or riding animal, any mechanically propelled cycle of any description, or any vessel commits an offence. The offender is liable to a fine not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for six months or to both.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Takes or converts to his use or to the use of any other person\", \"Property involved is a draught or riding animal, a mechanically propelled cycle of any description, or a vessel\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for six months or both.

fields.title: Conveying person by water for hire in unsafe or overloaded vessel
fields.description: A person who knowingly or negligently conveys, or causes to be conveyed for hire, any person by water in a vessel that is in such a state or so loaded as to be unsafe commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person conveys any person by water in a vessel or causes any person to be conveyed by water in a vessel\", \"Conveyance is for hire\", \"Vessel is in such a state or so loaded as to be unsafe\"]"]

fields.title: Counseling another to commit an offence
fields.description: Where a person counsels another to commit an offence and, after that counsel, the person counseled actually commits an offence, the counselor is treated as having counselled the offence actually committed if the facts constituting that offence were a probable consequence of carrying out the counsel. It is immaterial whether the offence committed is the same as that counselled or is committed in the same way.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person counsels another to commit an offence\", \"The person counseled subsequently actually commits an offence after the counsel\", \"The facts constituting the offence actually committed are a probable consequence of carrying out the counsel\"]"]

fields.title: Counselling to commit an offence
fields.description: A person who counsels another person to commit an offence is deemed to have taken part in committing that offence and may be charged either with committing the offence or with counselling (or procuring) its commission.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person counsels another person\", \"The counselling relates to the commission of a specified offence\"]"]

fields.title: Counterfeiting coins
fields.description: A person who makes or attempts to make any counterfeit coin commits an offence. The offence attracts a penalty of imprisonment for life.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Creates or manufactures (makes) a coin that is counterfeit\", \"Alternative conduct: attempts to make a counterfeit coin\"]"]

fields.title: Criminal trespass
fields.description: A person who unlawfully enters into or upon property in the possession of another, or who lawfully enters and then unlawfully remains there, with intent to commit an offence or to intimidate, insult or annoy the person in possession, commits criminal trespass.
fields.elements: ["[\"Entry into or upon property in the possession of another, or remaining there after lawful entry\", \"Entry or remaining is unlawful\", \"The property is in the possession of another person\", \"At the time of the entry or remaining, the accused had the requisite intent (see mental elements)\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for three months; if the property is a building, tent or vessel used as a human dwelling or a building used as a place of worship or as a place for the custody of property, imprisonment for one year.

fields.title: Cruelty to children
fields.description: A person who, having the custody, charge or care of any person under eighteen years of age, ill-treats, neglects or abandons that person, or causes, carries out or procures female genital mutilation, or procures that person to be assaulted, ill-treated, neglected or abandoned in a manner likely to cause suffering or injury to health (including loss or injury to sight, hearing, limb or organ, or mental derangement), commits the offence of cruelty to children.
fields.elements: ["[\"There is a person under eighteen years of age (victim)\", \"The accused has custody, charge or care of that person\", \"The accused ill-treats, neglects or abandons that person, or causes/carried out female genital mutilation, or procures that person to be assaulted, ill-treated, neglected or abandoned\", \"The conduct is in a manner likely to cause the person suffering or injury to health (including injury to or loss of sight or hearing, or limb or organ, or any mental derangement)\"]"]
fields.penalty: On conviction, a fine not exceeding one million shillings or imprisonment for a term of not less than five years and not exceeding fifteen years or both; and the court shall order the offender to pay compensation to the person in respect of whom the offence was committed for the injuries caused.

fields.title: Danger or obstruction in public way or line of navigation
fields.description: A person who, by doing any act or by omitting to take reasonable care with any property in his possession or under his charge, causes danger, obstruction or injury to any person in any public way or public line of navigation commits an offence. The person who commits the offence shall be liable to a fine.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person who does any act or omits to take reasonable care\", \"The act or omission relates to property in the accused's possession or under the accused's charge\", \"The act or omission causes danger, obstruction or injury to any person\", \"The danger, obstruction or injury occurs in any public way or public line of navigation\"]"]

fields.title: Deceiving witnesses
fields.description: A person who practises any fraud or deceit, or who knowingly makes or exhibits any false statement, representation, token or writing to any person called or to be called as a witness in any judicial proceeding, with intent to affect that person's testimony, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused practises any fraud or deceit, or knowingly makes or exhibits any false statement, representation, token or writing\", \"The act is directed to a person called or to be called as a witness\", \"The context is a judicial proceeding\", \"The act is done with intent to affect the testimony of that person as a witness\"]"]

fields.title: Defacing bank notes
fields.description: A person who, without authority, wilfully defaces, tears, cuts or otherwise mutilates any bank note or currency note which is legal tender commits an offence. On conviction the offender is liable to a fine of five hundred thousand shillings for each note defaced or, in default, to imprisonment for one year.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Prohibited act: wilfully defaces, tears, cuts or otherwise mutilates\", \"Object: any bank note or currency note which is legal tender\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine of five hundred thousand shillings for each note defaced or, in default, imprisonment for a term of one year.

fields.title: Defilement by husband of wife under eighteen
fields.description: A person who, being married to a woman under the age of eighteen years, has or attempts to have sexual intercourse with her, whether with or without her consent, before she has attained the age of eighteen years, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Accused is married to the woman\", \"The woman is under the age of eighteen years at the time\", \"Accused has sexual intercourse with her or attempts to do so\"]"]

fields.title: Defilement of idiots or imbeciles
fields.description: A person who, knowing a woman to be an idiot or imbecile, has or attempts to have unlawful sexual intercourse with her in circumstances not amounting to rape, but which prove that the offender knew at the time of the commission of the offence that the woman was an idiot or imbecile, commits an offence and is liable to imprisonment for fourteen years, with or without corporal punishment.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Victim: a woman who is an idiot or imbecile\", \"Act: has unlawful sexual intercourse with her or attempts to do so\", \"Knowledge: offender knows at the time that the woman is an idiot or imbecile\", \"Circumstances: the intercourse/attempt does not amount to rape but are such as to prove the offender's knowledge\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for fourteen years, with or without corporal punishment.

fields.title: Demanding property by written threats
fields.description: A person who, with intent to extort or gain anything and knowing the contents of a writing, causes any person to receive a writing that demands anything from any person and, without reasonable or probable cause, contains threats of injury or detriment to be caused to any person if the demand is not complied with, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Causes any person to receive a writing\", \"The writing demands anything from any person\", \"The writing contains threats of injury or detriment to be caused to any person if the demand is not complied with\", \"The demand is made without reasonable or probable cause\"]"]

fields.title: Demanding property upon forged testamentary instrument
fields.description: A person who, knowing that a testamentary instrument is forged or that probate or letters of administration were obtained by false evidence, procures the delivery or payment to themselves or another of any property or money by virtue of probate or letters of administration so granted or obtained commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person procures the delivery or payment of any property or money to themselves or any other person\", \"The delivery or payment is by virtue of probate or letters of administration\", \"The probate or letters of administration were either granted upon a forged testamentary instrument or obtained by false evidence\", \"The person knew that the testamentary instrument was forged or knew that the grant had been obtained by false evidence\"]"]
fields.penalty: Shall be liable to the same punishment as if he had forged the document by virtue whereof he procures the delivery or payment.

fields.title: Demanding property with menaces with intent to steal
fields.description: A person who, with intent to steal any valuable thing, demands it from another person by menaces or force commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for five years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused demanded a thing from another person\", \"The thing demanded was a valuable thing\", \"The demand was made with menaces or with force\", \"The accused acted with intent to steal the thing demanded\"]"]

fields.title: Desertion of children
fields.description: A person who, being the parent, guardian or other person having the lawful care or charge of a child whose apparent age is not more than eighteen years, and being able to maintain the child, wilfully and without lawful or reasonable cause deserts the child and leaves it without means of support commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Accused is the parent, guardian or other person having the lawful care or charge of a child\", \"The child\u2019s apparent age is not more than eighteen years\", \"Accused is able to maintain the child\", \"Accused wilfully deserts the child\", \"Accused leaves the child without means of support\", \"Desertion is without lawful or reasonable cause\"]"]

fields.title: Destroying evidence
fields.description: A person who, knowing that any book, document, device or thing is or may be required in evidence in a judicial proceeding, wilfully removes or destroys it or renders it illegible, undecipherable or incapable of identification, with intent to prevent it from being used in evidence, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor is a person\", \"The actor knows the book, document, device or thing is or may be required in evidence in a judicial proceeding\", \"The actor wilfully removes or destroys the item or renders it illegible, undecipherable or incapable of identification\", \"The actor acts with intent to prevent the item from being used in evidence\"]"]

fields.title: Destroying, falsifying, false entries or omission of material particulars in corporate books, documents or securities (with intent to defraud)
fields.description: Being a director, officer or member of a corporation or company, doing any of the following acts with intent to defraud—destroying, altering, mutilating or falsifying any book, document, valuable security or account belonging to the corporation or company (or any entry therein), making any false entry in any such book, document or account, or omitting any material particular from any such book, document or account—commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor is a director, officer or member of a corporation or company\", \"Actor destroys, alters, mutilates or falsifies any book, document, valuable security or account belonging to the corporation or company, or any entry in such book, document or account; or makes any false entry in any such book, document or account; or omits any material particular from any such book, document or account\", \"Actor is privy to the act if not the direct actor (privy to similar conduct counts)\", \"The act is done with intent to defraud\"]"]

fields.title: Detention in any premises with intent, or in brothel
fields.description: A person who detains a woman against her will in or upon any premises with intent that she may have unlawful sexual intercourse with any man, whether any particular man or generally, or who detains her in any brothel, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person (the accused) performs an act of detention\", \"The person detained is a woman\", \"Detention is against her will (lack of consent)\", \"Location/circumstance element: either (i) in or upon any premises with intent that she may have unlawful sexual intercourse with any man (whether any particular man or generally), or (ii) in any brothel\"]"]

fields.title: Disabling in order to commit offence
fields.description: A person who, by any means calculated to choke, suffocate or strangle, and with intent to commit or to facilitate the commission of an offence or to facilitate the flight of an offender after the commission or attempted commission of an offence, renders or attempts to render any person incapable of resistance commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Use of any means calculated to choke, suffocate or strangle\", \"Act that renders or attempts to render any person incapable of resistance\", \"Intent to commit or to facilitate the commission of an offence, or to facilitate the flight of an offender after the commission or attempted commission of an offence\"]"]

fields.title: Disinterring, dissecting or causing damage to a dead body without lawful authority or contrary to Minister's health rules
fields.description: A person who, without lawful authority in that behalf or otherwise than in accordance with rules made by the Minister responsible for health, disinters, dissects or causes damage to the dead body of any person commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Conduct: disinters, dissects, or causes damage to a dead body\", \"Object: the dead body of any person\", \"Absence of lawful authority or non\u2011compliance with rules made by the Minister responsible for health\"]"]

fields.title: Disobedience of lawful orders
fields.description: A person who disobeys any order, warrant or command duly made, issued or given by a court, or by an officer or person acting in any public capacity and duly authorised in that behalf, commits an offence. Unless another penalty or mode of proceeding is expressly prescribed for that disobedience, the offender is liable to imprisonment for two years.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person (the accused)\", \"Disobeys an order, warrant or command\", \"The order/warrant/command was duly made, issued or given\", \"The order was made by a court, or by an officer or person acting in any public capacity\", \"The court/officer/person was duly authorised in that behalf\"]"]

fields.title: Disobedience of statutory duty
fields.description: A person who wilfully contravenes any written law by doing an act which it forbids or by omitting to do an act which it requires, where the act or omission concerns the public or any part of the public, commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for two years unless a different punishment is provided by that or any other law.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Wilfully contravenes any written law\", \"By doing an act which the law forbids or omitting to do an act which the law requires\", \"The act or omission concerns the public or any part of the public\"]"]

fields.title: Disposing of girl under eighteen for intercourse with husband
fields.description: A person who, being a parent of or having custody of a woman under the age of eighteen years, parts with the possession of, or otherwise disposes of, the girl with the intention that she shall, while under eighteen years, have sexual intercourse with her husband, or who parts with possession knowing it likely she will while under eighteen have sexual intercourse, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Accused is a parent of or has custody of the woman\", \"The woman is under the age of eighteen years\", \"Accused parts with possession of or otherwise disposes of the girl\"]"]

fields.title: Dissuading persons from assisting with self-help schemes
fields.description: A person who, with intent to impede, obstruct, prevent or defeat any self-help scheme or type of self‑help scheme approved by the Regional Commissioner or the District Commissioner, dissuades or attempts to dissuade any person from offering his services, or from assisting, in connection therewith, commits an offence. On conviction the person is liable to a fine not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months or to both.
fields.elements: ["[\"a person (actor)\", \"there is a self-help scheme or type of self-help scheme approved by the Regional Commissioner or the District Commissioner\", \"the actor, with intent to impede, obstruct, prevent or defeat that scheme or type of scheme\", \"the actor dissuades or attempts to dissuade any person from offering his services or from assisting in connection with the scheme\"]"]
fields.penalty: a fine not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months or both

fields.title: Disturbing religious assemblies
fields.description: A person who voluntarily causes a disturbance to any assembly that is lawfully engaged in the performance of religious worship or a religious ceremony commits an offence. On conviction the person is liable to a fine of not less than three million shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding two years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Voluntary act that causes a disturbance\", \"Target: any assembly\", \"Assembly is lawfully engaged in the performance of religious worship or a religious ceremony\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine of not less than three million shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding two years.

fields.title: Employee engaged on a frolic of his own during work time without lawful excuse
fields.description: A person employed under lawful employment who, without lawful excuse, is found engaged on a frolic of his own at a time he is supposed to be engaged in activities connected with his employment shall be deemed an idle and disorderly person and liable to the prescribed penalty.
fields.elements: ["[\"The person is employed under lawful employment\", \"At a time the person is supposed to be engaged in employment activities, the person is found engaged on a frolic of his own\", \"There is no lawful excuse for being so engaged\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a period not exceeding three months or to both.

fields.title: Endangering safety of aviation
fields.description: A person who (a) performs an act of violence against a person on board an aircraft in flight likely to endanger the safety of the aircraft; (b) destroys an aircraft in service or causes damage rendering it incapable of flight or likely to endanger its safety in flight; (c) places on an aircraft in service a device or substance likely to destroy it or render it incapable of flight or likely to endanger its safety in flight; (d) destroys or damages air navigation facilities or interferes with their operation if likely to endanger the safety of aircraft in flight; or (e) communicates information which he knows to be false, thereby endangering the safety of an aircraft in flight, commits an offence.
