fields.elements: ["[\"an act by a person\", \"one or more prohibited acts: violence against a person on board an aircraft in flight; destruction or damaging of an aircraft in service rendering it incapable of flight or likely to endanger safety; placing a device or substance on an aircraft in service likely to destroy it or render it incapable of flight or likely to endanger safety; destroying or damaging air navigation facilities or interfering with their operation likely to endanger safety of aircraft in flight; communicating information known to be false thereby endangering safety of an aircraft in flight\", \"the act is of a kind that is likely to endanger the safety of the aircraft in flight or renders the aircraft incapable of flight\", \"the conduct falls within aircraft 'in flight' or 'in service' as defined in subsection (2) where relevant\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for a term not exceeding thirty years with corporal punishment.

fields.title: Endangering safety of persons travelling by railway (unlawful without intent)
fields.description: A person who unlawfully, but without the intent specified in subsection (1), causes the safety of any person travelling by railway to be endangered commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"a person (actor)\", \"an unlawful act or omission\", \"the act or omission causes the safety of any person travelling by railway to be endangered\"]"]

fields.title: Endangering safety of persons travelling by railway (with intent)
fields.description: A person who, with intent to injure or to endanger the safety of any person travelling by any railway (whether a particular person or not), performs any of several specified acts or omissions that endanger safety commits an offence punishable by imprisonment for life.
fields.elements: ["[\"a person (actor)\", \"intent to injure or to endanger the safety of any person travelling by any railway (may be a particular person or not)\", \"an act or omission causing the safety of that person travelling by railway to be endangered\"]"]

fields.title: Entering dwelling house with intent to commit offence
fields.description: A person who enters or is in any building, tent or vessel used as a human dwelling with intent to commit an offence therein commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for ten years, and if the offence is committed in the night the maximum term is fourteen years.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Enters or is in any building, tent or vessel\", \"The building, tent or vessel is used as a human dwelling\", \"With intent to commit an offence in that dwelling\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for ten years; if the offence is committed in the night, imprisonment for fourteen years.

fields.title: Escape from lawful custody
fields.description: Creates an offence where a person escapes from lawful custody. The provision penalises leaving or getting free from custody that is lawful at the time of the escape.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person was in lawful custody\", \"Person escaped from that custody\"]"]

fields.title: Exhibition of false light, mark or buoy
fields.description: A person who exhibits any false light, mark or buoy, intending or knowing it to be likely that its exhibition will mislead a navigator, commits an offence. The offence carries liability to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person (actor)\", \"Exhibits any false light, mark or buoy\", \"The exhibition is intended or known to be likely to mislead a navigator\"]"]

fields.title: Exporting counterfeit coin
fields.description: A person who, without lawful authority or excuse (the proof of which lies on him), exports or puts on board a vessel or vehicle for the purposes of being exported from Tanzania any counterfeit coin, knowing it to be counterfeit, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Exports or puts on board a vessel or vehicle of any kind\", \"The act is for the purposes of being exported from Tanzania\", \"The thing exported or placed on board is counterfeit coin\", \"The person knows the coin to be counterfeit\", \"The act is done without lawful authority or excuse\"]"]

fields.title: Exposure of wounds or deformation to obtain alms
fields.description: A person who wanders abroad and endeavors by exposing wounds or deformation to obtain or gather alms shall be deemed an idle and disorderly person and liable to the prescribed penalty.
fields.elements: ["[\"The person wanders abroad\", \"The person endeavors to obtain or gather alms by exposing wounds or deformation\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a period not exceeding three months or to both.

fields.title: Fabricating evidence
fields.description: A person who, with intent to mislead any tribunal in any judicial proceeding, fabricates evidence by any means other than perjury or subornation of perjury commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Fabricates evidence by any means other than perjury or subornation of perjury\", \"Act done with intent to mislead any tribunal\"]"]

fields.title: Failure to account for money collected by public subscription
fields.description: Any person who, having collected or procured to be collected money by subscription in Mainland Tanzania, when required by the District Commissioner or (for an urban authority) the police officer in charge of Police in that authority, fails to produce or publish full and correct accounts of money received by subscription and of its disposal shall be guilty of an offence. The first offence is punishable by imprisonment for two years and every subsequent offence by imprisonment for three years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person collected or procured to be collected money by subscription in Mainland Tanzania\", \"District Commissioner, or (for an urban authority) the police officer in charge of Police in that authority, requires the person to account\", \"Requirement to either produce full and correct accounts to the District Commissioner or police officer, or to publish them in a newspaper designated by that officer\", \"Person fails to comply with that requirement (does not produce or publish full and correct accounts of money received and of its disposal)\"]"]
fields.penalty: First offence: imprisonment for two years; every subsequent offence: imprisonment for three years.

fields.title: Failure to supply necessaries
fields.description: A person who, being charged with the duty of providing for another the necessaries of life, without lawful excuse fails to do so, whereby the life of that other person is or is likely to be endangered or his health is or is likely to be permanently injured, commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for three years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Accused is a person charged with the duty of providing for another the necessaries of life\", \"Accused, without lawful excuse, fails to provide those necessaries (an omission)\", \"By that failure, the other person's life is or is likely to be endangered, or the other person's health is or is likely to be permanently injured\"]"]

fields.title: False accounting by public officer
fields.description: A person who, being an officer charged with the receipt, custody or management of any part of the public revenue or property, knowingly furnishes any false statement or return about money or property received by him or entrusted to his care, or about any balance of money or property in his possession or under his control, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"the accused is a person who is an officer charged with the receipt, custody or management of any part of the public revenue or public property\", \"the accused furnished a statement or return\", \"the statement or return was false\", \"the false statement or return related to money or property received by the accused or entrusted to the accused's care, or to any balance of money or property in the accused's possession or under the accused's control\"]"]

fields.title: False assumption of authority to administer oaths or perform public acts
fields.description: A person who, without authority, assumes to act as someone having lawful authority to administer an oath, take a solemn declaration/affirmation/affidavit, or to do any other act of a public nature that can only be done by persons authorised by law, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"person\", \"acts without authority\", \"assumes to act as a person having authority by law to administer an oath or take a solemn declaration/affirmation/affidavit\", \"or assumes to do any other act of a public nature which can only be done by persons authorised by law\"]"]

fields.title: False assumption of judicial authority
fields.description: A person who is not a judicial officer but assumes to act as a judicial officer (except where acting as a customary arbitrator or tribal elder in specified customary-law matters) commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"person\", \"is not a judicial officer\", \"assumes to act as a judicial officer\"]"]

fields.title: False certificates by public officers
fields.description: A person who, being authorised or required by law to give any certificate touching any matter by virtue whereof the rights of any person may be prejudicially affected, gives a certificate which is, to his knowledge, false in any material particular commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person authorised or required by law to give a certificate\", \"The certificate touches a matter by virtue whereof the rights of any person may be prejudicially affected\", \"The accused gives a certificate\", \"The certificate is false in a material particular\", \"The accused knows the certificate is false in that material particular\"]"]

fields.title: False claims by officials
fields.description: A person employed in the public service in a capacity requiring or enabling them to furnish returns or statements about sums payable or other matters for payment or delivery who makes a return or statement which they know to be false in any material particular commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person is employed in the public service\", \"Employed in a capacity that requires or enables the person to furnish returns or statements\", \"Return or statement relates to any sum payable or claimed to be payable to the person or to any other person, or to any other matter required to be certified for the purpose of payment of money or delivery of goods\", \"Person makes such a return or statement\", \"The return or statement is false in a material particular and the person knows it to be false\"]"]

fields.title: False declaration for passport
fields.description: A person who, knowing a statement to be untrue, makes that statement for the purpose of procuring a passport for himself or for any other person commits an offence. On conviction the person is liable to a fine or imprisonment or both.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person makes a statement\", \"The statement is to the person's knowledge untrue\", \"The statement is made for the purpose of procuring a passport\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine of not less than one million shillings but not exceeding five million shillings, or imprisonment for a term of not less than two years but not exceeding five years, or both.

fields.title: False information to person employed in public service
fields.description: Whoever gives to any person employed in the public service any information which he knows or believes to be false, intending thereby to cause, or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby cause that person (a) to do or omit anything which that person ought not to do or omit if the true state of facts were known to him; or (b) to use his lawful power to the injury or annoyance of any person, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person gives information to a person employed in the public service\", \"The information given is false\", \"The accused knows or believes the information to be false\", \"The accused intends, or knows it is likely, that the information will cause the public servant to act (or omit to act) in a particular way\", \"That caused effect is either: (a) causing the public servant to do or omit something he ought not to do or omit if the true facts were known; or (b) causing the public servant to use his lawful power to the injury or annoyance of any person\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine of one hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for six months or both.

fields.title: Falsely acknowledging deeds, recognisances, etc
fields.description: A person who, without lawful authority or excuse (the proof of which lies on him), makes in the name of any other person, before any court or person lawfully authorised to take such an acknowledgement, an acknowledgement of liability of any kind or an acknowledgement of a deed or other instrument commits an offence under this section.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor is a person who makes an acknowledgement\", \"Acknowledgement is made in the name of some other person\", \"Acknowledgement is made before any court or person lawfully authorised to take such an acknowledgement\", \"The content of the acknowledgement is either: an acknowledgement of liability of any kind; or an acknowledgement of a deed or other instrument\", \"The actor has no lawful authority or excuse to make the acknowledgement (proof of excuse lies on the actor)\"]"]

fields.title: False statements by official of companies
fields.description: A person who, being a promoter, director, officer or auditor of a corporation or company (existing or intended to be formed), makes, circulates or publishes, or concurs in making, circulating or publishing, any written statement or account which is in any material particular to his knowledge false, with intent to effect certain specified purposes, commits an offence. The specified purposes include deceiving or defrauding members, shareholders or creditors, or inducing any person to become a member or to entrust, advance property to, or enter into security for the company.
fields.elements: ["[\"Accused is a promoter, director, officer or auditor of a corporation or company (existing or intended to be formed)\", \"Accused makes, circulates or publishes, or concurs in making, circulating or publishing\", \"A written statement or account is involved\", \"The statement or account is, in any material particular, false and known by the accused to be false\", \"The act is done with intent to effect one of the prohibited purposes (see alternatives)\"]"]

fields.title: False statements for registers of births, deaths and marriages
fields.description: A person who knowingly, and with intent that it be inserted in a register of births, deaths or marriages, makes any false statement touching any matter required by law to be registered in such a register commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person makes a statement touching a matter required by law to be registered\", \"The statement is false\", \"The person acts knowingly\", \"The person acts with intent that the false statement be procured/inserted in a register of births, deaths or marriages\"]"]

fields.title: False swearing
fields.description: A person who, on a matter of public concern, swears falsely or makes a false affirmation or declaration before a person authorised to administer an oath or take a declaration, under circumstances that would have amounted to perjury if made in a judicial proceeding, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Swears falsely or makes a false affirmation or declaration\", \"Statement made before a person authorised to administer an oath or take a declaration\", \"Statement concerns a matter of public concern\", \"Circumstances are such that, if the statement had been made in a judicial proceeding, it would have amounted to perjury\"]"]

fields.title: Falsification of register
fields.description: A person who, having actual custody of any register or record kept by lawful authority, knowingly permits any entry which in any material particular is to his knowledge false to be made in the register or record commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person having the actual custody of a register or record\", \"The register or record is kept by lawful authority\", \"The accused permits an entry to be made in the register or record\", \"The entry is false in a material particular\", \"The accused knows that the entry is false\"]"]

fields.title: Falsifying warrants for money payable under public authority
fields.description: A person employed in the public service who, knowingly and with intent to defraud, makes out or delivers to any person a warrant for the payment of any money payable by a public authority for a greater or lesser amount than that to which the person on whose behalf the warrant is made out is entitled commits an offence. The offender is liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Accused is employed in the public service\", \"Accused makes out or delivers a warrant for the payment of money\", \"The money is payable by a public authority\", \"The warrant is for a greater or lesser amount than that to which the person on whose behalf it is made is entitled\", \"The act is done knowingly and with intent to defraud\"]"]
fields.penalty: Liable to imprisonment for seven years.

fields.title: Forcible entry
fields.description: A person who, in order to take possession, enters on any land or tenements in a violent manner — whether by actual force applied to any other person, by threats, by breaking open any house, or by collecting an unusual number of people — commits the offence of forcible entry. It is immaterial whether the person is entitled to enter on the land or not.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person enters on any land or tenements\", \"The entry is made in a violent manner\", \"The entry is made in order to take possession of the land or tenements\"]"]

fields.title: Foreign enlistment
fields.description: A person commits an offence who, without the licence of the President, engages in specified acts that facilitate or involve military or naval service for a foreign state at war with any friendly state, or prepares expeditions against the dominions of a friendly state. The listed acts include preparing or fitting out expeditions, accepting or inducing acceptance of foreign commissions, quitting or boarding a vessel to take such service, knowingly taking illegally enlisted persons on board, and building, equipping or dispatching vessels or issuing commissions for such vessels.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"No licence: the acts are done without the licence of the President\", \"Prohibited conduct: performing one or more of the specified acts (see alternatives)\"]"]

fields.title: Forgery
fields.description: A person who forges any document commits an offence. Unless, owing to the circumstances of the forgery or the nature of the thing forged, some other punishment is provided, the offender is liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Prohibited act: forges\", \"Object: any document\"]"]

fields.title: Forgery of judicial or official document
fields.description: A person who forges any judicial or official document is guilty of an offence and is liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The existence of a document that is judicial or official in character\", \"The accused forged the document (made a false document or materially altered a document so as to make it false)\"]"]

fields.title: Forgery of specified instruments (punishable by imprisonment for life)
fields.description: A person who forges any will, document of title to land, judicial record, power of attorney, bank note, currency note, bill of exchange, promissory note or other negotiable instrument, policy of insurance, cheque or other authority for the payment of money by a banker is guilty of an offence. The person shall be liable to imprisonment for life and the court may in addition order that any such document be forfeited to the United Republic.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"forges (makes, alters or falsifies) a document or instrument\", \"the document or instrument is one of the specified types listed in the section\"]"]
fields.penalty: Liable to imprisonment for life; court may in addition order forfeiture of any such document to the United Republic.

fields.title: Forging a stamp
fields.description: A person who forges any stamp, whether impressed or adhesive, is liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"forges any stamp\", \"stamp may be impressed or adhesive\"]"]

fields.title: Fouling air
fields.description: A person who voluntarily vitiates the atmosphere in any place so as to make it noxious to the health of persons in general—whether dwelling or carrying on business in the neighbourhood or passing along a public way—commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor is a person\", \"Voluntary act of vitiating the atmosphere\", \"Location: in any place\", \"Result: atmosphere made noxious to the health of persons in general\"]"]

fields.title: Fouling water
fields.description: A person who voluntarily corrupts or fouls the water of any public spring or reservoir so as to render it less fit for the purpose for which it is ordinarily used commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person (actor) commits the act\", \"The act consists of corrupting or fouling water\", \"The water is of any public spring or reservoir\", \"The act renders the water less fit for the purpose for which it is ordinarily used\"]"]

fields.title: Fraud by public officer
fields.description: A person employed in the public service who, in the discharge of the duties of his office, commits any fraud affecting the public commits an offence. The provision applies whether the fraud would have been criminal or not if committed against a private person.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person is employed in the public service\", \"Act is done in the discharge of the duties of the person's office\", \"Person commits any fraud\", \"The fraud affects the public\"]"]

fields.title: Fraud on sale or mortgage of property
fields.description: A person who, being a seller or mortgagor of property or the advocate or agent of a seller or mortgagor, with intent to induce the purchaser or mortgagee to accept the title offered or produced to him, and with intent to defraud, conceals material instruments or encumbrances, falsifies pedigree on which the title depends, or makes false statements or conceals facts material to the title. Such conduct is an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Accused is a seller or mortgagor of property, or the advocate or agent of a seller or mortgagor\", \"There is an intention to induce the purchaser or mortgagee to accept the title offered or produced to them\", \"There is an intention to defraud\", \"The accused conceals, falsifies or makes false statements affecting the title or conceals facts/material instruments or encumbrances\"]"]

fields.title: Fraudulent appropriation of power
fields.description: A person who fraudulently abstracts or diverts to his own use or to the use of another any mechanical, illuminating or electrical power derived from a machine, apparatus or substance which is the property of another person commits an offence. The offender is liable to a fine of not less than five hundred thousand shillings but not exceeding one million shillings or to imprisonment for five years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Act: fraudulently abstracts or diverts power\", \"Object: mechanical, illuminating or electrical power\", \"Source: power derived from a machine, apparatus or substance\", \"Proprietary condition: the power is the property of another person\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine of not less than five hundred thousand shillings but not exceeding one million shillings or imprisonment for five years.

fields.title: Fraudulent disposition of mortgaged goods
fields.description: A person who, being the mortgagor of goods subject to a mortgage, removes or disposes of those goods without the consent of the mortgagee and with intent to defraud commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is the mortgagor of the goods\", \"The goods are mortgaged goods (subject to a valid charge or lien)\", \"The accused removes the goods or disposes of the goods\", \"The removal or disposition is without the consent of the mortgagee\", \"The act is done with intent to defraud\"]"]

fields.title: Fraudulent erasure or removal of particulars on stamped material to enable reuse
fields.description: A person who fraudulently erases or otherwise (really or apparently) removes from any stamped material any name, sum, date or other matter with intent that another use shall be made of the stamp upon that material is liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"fraudulently erases or otherwise really or apparently removes from any stamped material any name, sum, date or other matter or thing written thereon\", \"intent that another use shall be made of the stamp upon that material\"]"]

fields.title: Fraudulent erasure or removal of writing from an impressed or attached stamp with intent to use
fields.description: A person who fraudulently and with intent that use may be made of any stamp which has already been impressed upon or attached to any material erases or otherwise removes, really or apparently, from the material anything written on it, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person erases or otherwise removes (really or apparently) anything written on material to which a stamp has been impressed or attached\", \"Conduct is fraudulent\", \"There is intent that the stamp may be used\"]"]

fields.title: Fraudulent fixing or placing of removed stamp or stamp part onto material
fields.description: A person who fraudulently fixes or places upon any material or upon any stamp any stamp or part of a stamp which has been removed from other material or cut off from another stamp commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person fixes or places a stamp or part of a stamp upon material or upon any stamp\", \"The stamp or part was previously removed from other material or cut off from another stamp\", \"Conduct is fraudulent\"]"]

fields.title: Fraudulently cutting, tearing or removing a stamp to enable another use
fields.description: A person who fraudulently cuts, tears in any way, or removes from any material any stamp with intent that another use shall be made of the stamp or any part of it is liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"fraudulently cuts, tears in any way, or removes from any material any stamp\", \"intent that another use shall be made of the stamp or any part of it\"]"]

fields.title: Fraudulently dealing with minerals in mines
fields.description: A person who takes, conceals, or otherwise disposes of any ore or any metal or mineral in or about a mine, with intent to defraud any person, commits an offence and is liable to imprisonment for five years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person takes, conceals, or otherwise disposes of any ore or any metal or mineral\", \"The act occurs in or about a mine\", \"The act is done with intent to defraud any person\"]"]

fields.title: Fraudulently fixing or placing a removed or detached stamp or part
fields.description: A person who fraudulently fixes or places upon any material or upon any stamp a stamp or part of a stamp which, whether fraudulently or not, has been cut, torn or removed from any other material or out of or from any other stamp is liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"fraudulently fixes or places upon any material or upon any stamp a stamp or part of a stamp\", \"the stamp or part has been cut, torn or removed from other material or from another stamp (whether fraudulently or not)\"]"]

fields.title: Fraudulent mutilation of a stamp
fields.description: A person who fraudulently mutilates any stamp with intent that another use shall be made of the stamp is liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"fraudulently mutilates any stamp\", \"intent that another use shall be made of the stamp\"]"]

fields.title: Fraudulent mutilation of a stamp with intent to use part of it
fields.description: A person who fraudulently mutilates a stamp with intent that use may be made of any part of the stamp commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person mutilates a stamp\", \"Mutilation is fraudulent\", \"There is intent that use may be made of any part of the stamp\"]"]

fields.title: Fraudulent or false accounting by clerk
fields.description: A person who, being a clerk or servant or employed or acting in that capacity, with intent to defraud destroys, alters, mutilates or falsifies any book, document, valuable security or account belonging to or in the possession of his employer (or received by him on account of his employer), or makes or is privy to making any false entry in such a book, document or account, or omits or is privy to omitting any material particular from any such book, document or account, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Accused is a clerk or servant or is employed or acting in the capacity of a clerk or servant\", \"Accused did one or more of the prohibited acts (destroy, alter, mutilate, falsify, make false entry, omit material particular) in relation to a book, document, valuable security or account\", \"The book, document, valuable security or account belongs to or is in the possession of the accused's employer, or has been received by the accused on account of his employer, or relates to an entry in such a book, document or account\", \"The act was done with intent to defraud\"]"]

fields.title: Fraudulent pretence of marriage
fields.description: A person who wilfully and by fraud causes a woman who is not lawfully married to him to believe she is lawfully married to him, and who thereby induces her to cohabit or have sexual intercourse with him in that belief, commits an offence. The offender is liable to imprisonment for ten years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused wilfully and by fraud caused a woman to believe she was lawfully married to him\", \"The woman was not lawfully married to the accused\", \"The woman, in that belief, cohabited with the accused or had sexual intercourse with him\"]"]

fields.title: Fraudulent removal of stamp from material with intent to use it
fields.description: A person who fraudulently and with intent that the stamp or any part of it may be used removes a stamp from any material in any way commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person removes a stamp from any material\", \"Removal is fraudulent\", \"There is intent that the stamp or any part of it may be used\"]"]

fields.title: Gang rape
fields.description: Where the offence of rape is committed by one or more persons in a group, each person in the group who commits or abets the commission of the offence is deemed to have committed gang rape. Persons convicted of gang rape are subject to the penalties set out in this section.
fields.elements: ["[\"There is an underlying offence of rape committed\", \"The rape is committed by one or more persons in a group\", \"The accused either committed the rape or abetted its commission as a member of that group\"]"]

fields.title: Gang robbery
fields.description: Where two or more persons steal anything, and at or immediately before or immediately after stealing use or threaten to use actual violence to any person or property in order to obtain or retain the thing stolen, they commit the offence of gang robbery.
fields.elements: ["[\"Two or more persons commit a theft (steal anything)\", \"At or immediately before or immediately after the time of stealing\", \"One or more of them use or threaten to use actual violence\", \"The violence or threat is to any person or to property\", \"The use/threat is in order to obtain or retain the thing stolen\"]"]

fields.title: Going armed in public
fields.description: A person who goes armed in public without lawful occasion in such a manner as to cause terror to any person commits an offence. The person's arms may be forfeited.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person goes armed in public\", \"No lawful occasion for being armed\", \"Conduct is in such a manner as to cause terror to any person\"]"]

fields.title: Grave sexual abuse
fields.description: A person who, for sexual gratification, performs any act by use of his genital or any other part of the human body or any instrument, or any orifice or part of the body of another person, that does not amount to rape under section 130, commits the offence of grave sexual abuse where the act occurs in any of the specified circumstances (a)–(d).
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person who performs the act\", \"Conduct: does any act for sexual gratification by use of his genital or any other part of the human body, any instrument, or any orifice or part of the body of another person\", \"Limitation: the act does not amount to rape under section 130\", \"Circumstance: the act occurs in at least one of the statute's specified circumstances\"]"]
fields.penalty: On conviction, imprisonment for a term of not less than eighteen years and not exceeding thirty years, with corporal punishment, and an order to pay compensation of an amount determined by the court to the person in respect of whom the offence was committed.

fields.title: Grievous harm
fields.description: A person who unlawfully does grievous harm to another commits an offence. The offender is liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person (the actor)\", \"Unlawfully does grievous harm\", \"To another person (the victim)\"]"]

fields.title: Habitual dealing in slaves
fields.description: A person who habitually imports, exports, removes, buys, sells, traffics or deals in slaves commits an offence. The offender is liable to imprisonment for ten years.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Habitual commission of one or more prohibited acts\", \"Prohibited acts: importing, exporting, removing, buying, selling, trafficking or dealing in slaves\", \"Object/target: slaves (persons in slavery)\"]"]

fields.title: Handling of poisonous substance in negligent manner
fields.description: A person who, with any poisonous substance, does any act in a manner so rash or negligent as to endanger human life or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person, or who knowingly or negligently omits to take such care with any poisonous substance in his possession as is necessary to guard against probable danger to human life, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Existence of a poisonous substance (in relation to the actor)\", \"The accused does an act with the poisonous substance, or omits to take necessary care with a poisonous substance in his possession\", \"The act is done in a manner so rash or negligent as to endanger human life or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person; or the omission is knowing or negligent and fails to guard against probable danger to human life\"]"]
fields.penalty: Fine of two hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for six months.

fields.title: Harbouring common prostitutes
fields.description: Being the keeper of a bar, hotel, house, shop, room or other place of public resort for the sale or consumption of refreshments, knowingly permitting or suffering common prostitutes to assemble at and remain on the premises for the purpose of prostitution is an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Accused is the keeper of a bar, hotel, house, shop, room or other place of public resort for the sale or consumption of refreshments\", \"Accused knowingly permits or suffers persons who are common prostitutes to assemble at and remain on the premises\", \"The persons assemble and remain on the premises for the purpose of prostitution\"]"]
fields.penalty: On conviction, liable to a fine not exceeding fifty thousand shillings; for a second or subsequent offence, a fine not exceeding five hundred thousand shillings.

fields.title: Hate speech
fields.description: A person who uses threatening, abusive or insulting words or behaviour (including displays of written material), publishes or distributes written material, presents or directs the (public) performance of a play, distributes/shows/plays recordings of audio or visual images, or provides/produces/directs a programme that is threatening, abusive or insulting, commits an offence if the person intends thereby to stir up ethnic hatred or, having regard to all the circumstances, ethnic hatred is likely to be stirred up. "Hatred" is against a group defined by reference to colour, race, gender, disability, conscience, belief, nationality or ethnic or national origins.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused engaged in one or more prohibited acts: using threatening, abusive or insulting words or behaviour or displaying written material; publishing or distributing written material; presenting or directing the (public) performance of a play; distributing, showing or playing a recording of audio or visual images; directing a public performance of a play; or providing/producing/directing a programme that is threatening, abusive or insulting or involves such words or behaviour\", \"The conduct was committed with the intention of stirring up ethnic hatred, OR having regard to all the circumstances ethnic hatred was likely to be stirred up\", \"The hatred in question is against a group defined by reference to colour, race, gender, disability, conscience, belief, nationality or ethnic or national origins\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine of five million shillings or imprisonment for a term not less than five years or to both.

fields.title: Hoarding of commodities
fields.description: A person, not being a manufacturer or producer of the commodity, commits the offence of hoarding where they (a) are found in possession of a commodity (value of one million shillings or less) in demand in the locality and it can be inferred it is not displayed or stocked for lawful sale or available at the lawful price; (b) sell or offer a commodity for sale at an unlawful or, having regard to all relevant circumstances, unreasonable price or on unlawful/unreasonable terms; or (c) in carrying on the business of selling a commodity, engage in trade practices intended or likely to create an artificial shortage, aggravate an existing shortage, cause an unlawful change in the official/commercial price, or adversely affect fair distribution among purchasers.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person (whether or not licensed to sell) who is not a manufacturer or producer of the commodity\", \"One or more prohibited acts in relation to a commodity: possession (as described), unlawful/unreasonable sale/offer, or prohibited trade practices (listed)\", \"Commodity is in demand by the public in the locality concerned (for the possession limb)\", \"Possession may be inferred to be not for lawful sale or not available at the lawful price (possession limb)\"]"]
fields.penalty: On conviction, imprisonment for a term not exceeding fourteen years or a fine not exceeding fifty thousand shillings or both; court may order the commodity forfeited to the Government unless another person satisfies the court that the commodity belonged to him and that person is not guilty of an offence under this section.

fields.title: Housebreaking
fields.description: A person who breaks and enters any building, tent or vessel used as a human dwelling with intent to commit an offence therein; or who, having entered such a dwelling with intent to commit an offence or having committed an offence therein, breaks out of it, commits housebreaking and is liable to imprisonment for fourteen years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Entry by breaking into a building, tent or vessel used as a human dwelling, or breaking out after having entered\", \"The building, tent or vessel is used as a human dwelling\", \"The entry/break-out is connected to an intent to commit an offence in the dwelling or occurs after having committed an offence there\"]"]

fields.title: House holder, etc., permitting defilement of girl on his premises
fields.description: A person who, being the owner or occupier of premises or having or acting or assisting in the management or control thereof, induces or knowingly suffers any woman under the age of fifteen years to resort to or be upon those premises for the purpose of sexual intercourse with any man (whether with any particular man or generally) commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is the owner or occupier of premises, or has, is acting in, or assists in the management or control of the premises\", \"The accused induces or knowingly suffers a woman under the age of fifteen years to resort to or be upon those premises\", \"The resorting or being upon the premises is for the purpose of sexual intercourse with any man (whether a particular man or generally)\"]"]

fields.title: House holder etc., permitting defilement of girl under eighteen years of age on his premises
fields.description: A person who, being the owner or occupier of premises or having or acting or assisting in the management or control thereof, induces or knowingly suffers any woman above the age of fifteen years and under the age of eighteen years to resort to or be upon the premises for the purpose of unlawful sexual intercourse with a man commits an offence under this section.
fields.elements: ["[\"Accused is the owner or occupier of the premises, or has, acts, or assists in the management or control of the premises\", \"Accused induces or knowingly suffers a woman to resort to or be upon the premises\", \"The woman is above the age of fifteen years and under the age of eighteen years\", \"The woman is on the premises for the purpose of unlawful sexual intercourse with a man (whether intended with any particular man or generally)\"]"]

fields.title: Idle and disorderly person — common prostitute behaving disorderly, loitering or soliciting in public
fields.description: A common prostitute who behaves in a disorderly or indecent manner in a public place, or who loiters or solicits in a public place for the purposes of prostitution, shall be deemed an idle and disorderly person and liable to the prescribed penalty.
fields.elements: ["[\"The person is a common prostitute\", \"The person either (a) behaves in a disorderly or indecent manner in a public place, or (b) loiters or solicits in a public place for the purposes of prostitution\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a period not exceeding three months or to both.

fields.title: Importing, conveying, exporting or putting in circulation obscene matter for trade, distribution or public exhibition
fields.description: A person who, for the purpose of or by way of trade or for the purpose of distribution or public exhibition, imports, conveys or exports, or causes to be imported, conveyed or exported any such matters or things, or in any manner whatsoever puts any of them in circulation commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Imports, conveys or exports, or causes to be imported, conveyed or exported obscene writings, images, films or other obscene objects or puts them in circulation\", \"Act is for the purpose of or by way of trade or for the purpose of distribution or public exhibition (as in paragraph (a))\"]"]
fields.penalty: Fine of two hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for two years.

fields.title: Importing, selling or possessing for sale prescribed uniforms, buttons or badges without permission
fields.description: A person who, not being in the service of the United Republic or not having previously received the written permission of the Inspector‑General of Police, imports, sells or has in his possession for sale any uniform or the buttons or badges appropriate to it commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Not being in the service of the United Republic or not having previously received the written permission of the Inspector\u2011General of Police\", \"Imports or sells or has in his possession for sale any uniform or the buttons or badges appropriate to it\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine of two hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for six months.

fields.title: Incest by females
fields.description: A female person aged 18 years or older who, with consent, permits her grandfather, father, brother or son to have carnal knowledge of her, knowing him to be that relative, commits the offence of incest. The offender is liable to imprisonment for life or to imprisonment of not less than thirty years and shall, in addition, be ordered to pay compensation to the victim as determined by the court.
fields.elements: ["[\"Offender is a female person of or above the age of eighteen years\", \"Offender permits a male person to have carnal knowledge of her\", \"The male person is the offender's grandfather, father, brother or son\", \"Offender knows the male to be her grandfather, father, brother or son\", \"Permission/consent is given by the female\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for life or for imprisonment of not less than thirty years; and ordered to pay compensation to the victim as determined by the court.

fields.title: Incest (male with female close relative)
fields.description: A male person who has prohibited sexual intercourse with a female person who is, to his knowledge, his granddaughter, daughter, sister or mother, commits the offence of incest. On conviction, the offender is liable to a term of imprisonment (minimum terms vary by the age of the female).
fields.elements: ["[\"Offender is a male person\", \"Offender has sexual intercourse with a female person\", \"The female is the offender's granddaughter, daughter, sister or mother\"]"]
fields.penalty: If the female is under 18: imprisonment for a term of not less than thirty years; if the female is 18 or more: imprisonment for a term of not less than twenty years.

fields.title: Inciting members of security or law enforcement organs to sedition or disobedience
fields.description: A person who incites any member of the Police Force, Prisons Service, National Service or any other law enforcement organ to sedition or to disobedience to any lawful order given by a superior officer commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person incites\", \"The target is a member of the Police Force, Prisons Service, National Service, or any other law enforcement organ\", \"The incitement is to sedition or to disobedience of a lawful order given by a superior officer\"]"]

fields.title: Inciting mutiny
fields.description: A person who advisedly attempts to effect any of the following purposes—(a) to seduce any member of the Police Force, the Prisons Service, the National Service or any other law enforcement organ from his duty and allegiance to the United Republic; (b) to incite such a person to commit an act of mutiny or any traitorous or mutinous act; or (c) to incite such a person to make or endeavour to make a mutinous assembly—commits an offence and is liable to imprisonment for life.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person who advisedly attempts to effect one of the prohibited purposes\", \"Target is a member of the Police Force, the Prisons Service, the National Service or any other law enforcement organ\", \"One prohibited purpose is to seduce that member from his duty and allegiance to the United Republic\", \"One prohibited purpose is to incite that member to commit an act of mutiny or any traitorous or mutinous act\", \"One prohibited purpose is to incite that member to make or endeavour to make a mutinous assembly\"]"]

fields.title: Indecent assault of boys under eighteen
fields.description: A person who unlawfully and indecently assaults a boy under the age of eighteen years commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for life.
fields.elements: ["[\"An actor (person) who commits an assault\", \"The assault is indecent in nature\", \"The assault is unlawful (no lawful justification)\", \"The victim is a boy under the age of eighteen years\"]"]

fields.title: Indecent practices between males
fields.description: A male person who, whether in public or private, commits any act of gross indecency with another male person, procures another male to commit any act of gross indecency with him, or attempts to procure the commission of any such act by any male person (with himself or with another male), commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Accused is a male person\", \"There is another male person involved\", \"Accused commits any act of gross indecency with another male person; or\", \"Accused procures another male person to commit any act of gross indecency with him; or\", \"Accused attempts to procure the commission of any such act by any male person, with himself or with another male person\"]"]

fields.title: Inducing soldiers or police officers to desert
fields.description: A person who, by any means whatsoever, directly or indirectly, procures or persuades or attempts to procure or persuade a member of the Police Force, Prisons Service, National Service or any other law enforcement organ to desert; or aids, abets or is accessory to such desertion; or, having reason to believe someone is a deserter, harbours or aids in concealing that person, commits an offence. Such an offender is liable to imprisonment for six months.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person who, by any means whatsoever, directly or indirectly, engages in one of the prohibited acts\", \"Prohibited acts (any one suffices): procures or persuades or attempts to procure or persuade a member to desert; aids, abets or is accessory to the desertion of a member; or harbours or aids in concealing a person who is a deserter\", \"The target of the prohibited act is any member of the Police Force, Prisons Service, National Service or any other law enforcement organ (i.e. the person who deserts or is a deserter)\"]"]

fields.title: Injuring animals
fields.description: A person who wilfully and unlawfully kills, maims or wounds any animal capable of being stolen commits an offence. The provision criminalises intentionally causing death or serious physical injury to specified animals without lawful excuse.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person (the accused) performs an act\", \"The act results in the animal being killed, maimed or wounded\", \"The animal is capable of being stolen\"]"]

fields.title: Insulting religion
fields.description: A person who destroys, damages or defiles any place of worship or any object held sacred by any class of persons, with the intention of insulting the religion of any class of persons or with the knowledge that any class of persons is likely to consider such conduct an insult to their religion, commits an offence. The provision covers acts against places of worship and sacred objects and requires either intent to insult or knowledge that the act will likely be considered insulting.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Prohibited conduct: destroys, damages or defiles\", \"Target: any place of worship or any object held sacred by any class of persons\", \"Mental element: intention to insult the religion of any class of persons or knowledge that such conduct is likely to be considered an insult to their religion\"]"]

fields.title: Intentional and unauthorised sharing by communication of photos, pictures or videos of corpses, dead persons, victims of crimes or gruesome incidents
fields.description: Prohibits intentionally and without authority using any form of communication to share pictures, videos or photos of corpses, dead persons, victims of crimes or gruesome incidents. Contravention of this prohibition is an offence punishable as provided in subsection (3).
fields.elements: ["[\"a person\", \"used any form of communication to share pictures, videos or photos\", \"the images shared depicted corpses, dead persons, victims of crimes or gruesome incidents\", \"the sharing was done without authority\"]"]
fields.penalty: On conviction, liable to a fine of not less than one million shillings or to imprisonment for a term of not less than one year or to both.

fields.title: Interference with witnesses
fields.description: A person who, in order to obstruct the due course of justice, dissuades, hinders or prevents any person lawfully bound to appear and give evidence as a witness from so appearing and giving evidence, or attempts to do so, commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for five years.
fields.elements: ["[\"An act of dissuading, hindering or preventing a person lawfully bound to appear and give evidence from so appearing and giving evidence\", \"Or an attempt to carry out such an act\", \"Purpose/circumstance: done in order to obstruct the due course of justice\"]"]

fields.title: Intimidation
fields.description: A person who wrongfully and without legal authority intimidates any other person with a view to compelling that other person either to abstain from doing any act which he has a legal right to do or to do any act which he has a legal right to abstain from doing commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Wrongfully and without legal authority (conduct must be wrongful and not legally authorised)\", \"Act: intimidates any other person\", \"Purpose: with a view to compelling that other person to (a) abstain from doing any act which he has a legal right to do, or (b) do any act which he has a legal right to abstain from doing\", \"Meaning of 'to intimidate': to cause in the mind of a person a reasonable apprehension of injury to him or to any member of his family or any of his dependants or of violence or damage to any person or property\", \"Meaning of 'injury': includes injury to a person in respect of his business, occupation, employment or other source of income and includes any actionable wrong\"]"]

fields.title: Issuing adulterated drugs from a dispensary as unadulterated
fields.description: A person who, knowing any drug or medical preparation to have been adulterated in such a manner as to lessen its efficacy, to change its operation, or to render it noxious, issues it from any dispensary for medicinal purposes as unadulterated commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor is a person\", \"The article is a drug or medical preparation\", \"The drug/medical preparation has been adulterated (so as to lessen efficacy, change operation, or render it noxious)\", \"The person knows of the adulteration\", \"The person issues the drug/medical preparation from a dispensary for medicinal purposes as unadulterated\"]"]

fields.title: Keeping a common gaming house
fields.description: A person who keeps a common gaming house commits an offence. A person is said to keep a common gaming house if they (a) as owner, occupier or person having the use of any house, room or place, open, keep or use it for the purpose of unlawful gaming being carried on therein, (b) as owner or occupier knowingly and wilfully permit it to be opened, kept or used by another for that purpose, or (c) have the care or management of, or in any manner assist in conducting the business of, such a place.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person (natural person)\", \"Keeps a common gaming house (as defined by subsection (1))\", \"Place: any house, room or place opened, kept or used for the purpose of unlawful gaming\"]"]

fields.title: Kidnapping
fields.description: A person who kidnaps any other person by taking them from Mainland Tanzania or by taking them from lawful guardianship commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused kidnapped another person\", \"The victim was taken from Mainland Tanzania or from lawful guardianship\"]"]

fields.title: Kidnapping or abducting child with intent to steal
fields.description: A person who kidnaps or abducts any child under the age of eighteen years with the intention of dishonestly taking any movable property from the person of the child commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Act: kidnaps or abducts the child\", \"Victim: any child under the age of eighteen years\", \"Mental element: intention to take dishonestly\", \"Property: any movable property taken from the person of the child\"]"]

fields.title: Kidnapping or abducting in order to murder
fields.description: A person who kidnaps or abducts another person with the purpose that the person may be murdered, or may be so disposed of as to be put in danger of being murdered, commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for ten years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused kidnaps or abducts another person\", \"The kidnapping or abduction is carried out in order that the person may be murdered\", \"Or the kidnapping or abduction is carried out in order that the person may be so disposed of as to be put in danger of being murdered\"]"]

fields.title: Kidnapping or abducting with intent to confine
fields.description: A person who kidnaps or abducts another person with intent to cause that person to be secretly and wrongfully confined commits an offence. The offender is liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused kidnapped or abducted a person (actus reus)\", \"The accused intended that the person be secretly and wrongfully confined (mens rea)\"]"]

fields.title: Kidnapping or abducting with intent to do harm
fields.description: A person who kidnaps or abducts another in order that the other person may be subjected, or may be so disposed of as to be put in danger of being subjected, to grievous harm, or slavery, or to the unnatural lust of any person, or who knows it to be likely that the person will be so subjected or disposed of, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused kidnaps or abducts another person\", \"The act is done either (a) in order that the other person may be subjected to grievous harm, slavery, or the unnatural lust of any person, or (b) in order that the other person may be so disposed of as to be put in danger of being subjected to grievous harm, slavery, or the unnatural lust of any person\", \"Alternatively, the accused knows it to be likely that the person will be so subjected or disposed of\"]"]

fields.title: Killing animals with intent to steal
fields.description: A person who kills an animal capable of being stolen, other than an animal to which section 268 applies, with intent to steal its skin or carcass, or any part of its skin or carcass, commits an offence. On conviction the person is liable to the same punishment as if they had stolen the animal.
fields.elements: ["[\"An act of killing an animal\", \"The animal is capable of being stolen\", \"The killing is done with intent to steal the animal's skin or carcass, or any part of its skin or carcass\"]"]
fields.penalty: Liable to the same punishment as if he had stolen the animal.

fields.title: Kite-flying
fields.description: Subject to subsection (3), a person who fraudulently obtains credit or money of any amount from a banker by means of kite-flying commits an offence. On conviction the person is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding ten years.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"fraudulently obtains credit or money of any amount\", \"from a banker\", \"by means of kite-flying\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for a term not exceeding ten years.

fields.title: Kite-flying (assistance by bank employee)
fields.description: A person employed by a bank who, by virtue of his office, fraudulently assists another person to obtain credit or money of any amount from a bank by means of kite-flying commits an offence. On conviction the person is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding ten years.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person employed by a bank (actor)\", \"by virtue of his office\", \"fraudulently assists another person\", \"to obtain credit or money of any amount\", \"from a bank\", \"by means of kite-flying\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for a term not exceeding ten years.

fields.title: Knowingly uttering a crossed cheque with obliterated, added or altered crossing
fields.description: A person who, with intent to defraud, knowingly utters a crossed cheque the crossing on which has been obliterated, added to or altered, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person\", \"There is a crossed cheque\", \"The crossing on the cheque has been obliterated, added to or altered\", \"The accused utters the crossed cheque\", \"The act is done with intent to defraud\"]"]

fields.title: Lending, etc., certificate for personation
fields.description: A person to whom a document has been issued by lawful authority certifying that he possesses a qualification recognised by law, or is the holder of an office, or is entitled to exercise a profession, trade or business, or to any right, privilege, rank or status, who sells, gives or lends that document to another with intent that the other may represent himself as the person named in the document, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person to whom a document has been issued by lawful authority\", \"The document certifies possession of a qualification recognised by law, or that the holder occupies an office, or is entitled to exercise a profession, trade or business, or is entitled to any right, privilege, rank or status\", \"The accused sells, gives or lends the document to another person\", \"The accused intends that the other person may represent himself as the person named in the document\"]"]

fields.title: Lending, selling or giving testimonial for personation
fields.description: A person who, being a person to whom any document mentioned in section 373 has been given, gives, sells or lends that document to another person with intent that the other person may utter the document for the purpose of obtaining any employment, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Accused is a person to whom a document referred to in section 373 has been given\", \"Accused gives, sells or lends that document to another person\", \"Accused intends that the other person may utter the document\", \"The utterance is for the purpose of obtaining any employment\"]"]

fields.title: Making documents without authority
fields.description: A person who, with intent to defraud or to deceive, (a) without lawful authority or excuse makes, signs or executes for or in the name or account of another person any document or writing; or (b) knowingly utters any document or writing so made, signed or executed by another person, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused made, signed or executed for or in the name or account of another person any document or writing without lawful authority or excuse; or knowingly uttered any document or writing so made, signed or executed by another person\", \"The conduct was done with intent to defraud or to deceive\"]"]

fields.title: Making, mending, using or possessing dies, plates or instruments for making stamps
fields.description: Without lawful authority or excuse, a person who makes, mends, begins or prepares to make or mend, uses, knowingly has in his possession, or disposes of any die, plate or instrument used for the purpose of making any stamp (impressed or adhesive) for the public revenue, the Tanzania Posts Corporation or any foreign country, or capable of producing the marks used on authorized stamp paper, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person acts in relation to a die, plate or instrument\", \"The item is used for making a stamp (impressed or adhesive) used for public revenue, Tanzania Posts Corporation, or any foreign country, or is capable of producing words/figures/letters/marks/lines used on authorized stamp paper\", \"Act consists of making, mending, beginning or preparing to make or mend, using, knowingly possessing, or disposing of the item\", \"No lawful authority or excuse (proof on the accused)\"]"]

fields.title: Making or having in possession paper or implements for forgery
fields.description: Without lawful authority or excuse (proof whereof lies on the accused), a person who makes, uses or knowingly has in custody or possession specified paper, plates, frames, moulds, instruments or printed material intended to resemble or reproduce features of currency or documents evidencing title to stock or shares commits an offence. The section lists several alternative acts (making, using, possessing, engraving, or having engraved plates or printed paper) that attract liability.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused acted without lawful authority or excuse (proof of lawful authority or excuse lies upon him)\", \"The accused did one or more of the prohibited acts set out in the section (see alternatives): made; used; knowingly had in custody or possession; engraved or otherwise made plates/blocks; used or knowingly had plates or paper on which reproductions were made\", \"The object of the act was paper, plates, frames, moulds, instruments, or printed material intended to resemble and pass as special paper used for currency or bank notes, or to produce words, figures, letters, marks, lines or devices peculiar to such notes or to documents evidencing title to public or corporate stock, annuities, funds or debts\"]"]

fields.title: Making or possessing dies or instruments for making stamp impressions without lawful excuse
fields.description: A person who, without lawful excuse (proof whereof lies on him), makes or knowingly has in his possession any die or instrument capable of making the impression of any stamp is liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"makes any die or instrument capable of making the impression of any stamp; or\", \"knowingly has in his possession any such die or instrument\"]"]

fields.title: Making use of fabricated evidence
fields.description: A person who, with intent to mislead any tribunal in any judicial proceeding, knowingly makes use of fabricated evidence commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Knowingly makes use of fabricated evidence\", \"Act done with intent to mislead any tribunal\"]"]

fields.title: Male person living on earnings of prostitution or persistently soliciting
fields.description: A male person who knowingly lives wholly or in part on the earnings of prostitution, or who in any public place persistently solicits or importunes for immoral purposes, commits an offence. On a second or subsequent conviction under this section the court may, in addition to any term of imprisonment awarded, sentence the offender to corporal punishment.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: male person\", \"Knowingly lives wholly or in part on the earnings of prostitution; or\", \"In any public place persistently solicits or importunes for immoral purposes\", \"Proof of either alternative is sufficient to constitute the offence\"]"]
fields.penalty: On conviction the offender may be sentenced to a term of imprisonment; on a second or subsequent conviction the court may, in addition to any term of imprisonment awarded, sentence the offender to corporal punishment.

fields.title: Maliciously administering poison with intent to harm
fields.description: A person who, unlawfully and with intent to injure or annoy another, causes any poison or noxious thing to be administered to, or taken by, any person, and thereby endangers that person’s life or does them grievous harm, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Causing any poison or noxious thing to be administered to, or taken by, any person\", \"The act is unlawful\", \"The accused acted with intent to injure or annoy another\", \"By that act the victim\u2019s life is endangered or the victim suffers grievous harm\"]"]

fields.title: Manslaughter (by excessive force in defence)
fields.description: A person who causes the death of another as the result of excessive force used in defence is guilty of manslaughter. The provision applies where the force used in exercising a defence (such as self-defence, defence of another, or defence of property) is excessive and causes death.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person causes the death of another person\", \"The death is the result of force used by the accused\", \"The force was used in the context of a defence (e.g. self-defence, defence of another, or defence of property)\", \"The force used was excessive for the defence being exercised\"]"]

fields.title: Marriage ceremony fraudulently gone through without lawful marriage
fields.description: A person who dishonestly or with a fraudulent intention goes through the ceremony of marriage, knowing that he is not thereby lawfully married, commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for five years.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Goes through the ceremony of marriage\", \"Does so dishonestly or with a fraudulent intention\", \"Knows that he is not thereby lawfully married\"]"]

fields.title: Melting down of coin
fields.description: A person who melts down, breaks up or defaces by stamping thereon any name, word or mark any coin current in Tanzania commits an offence. On conviction the person is liable to a fine of fifty thousand shillings or to imprisonment for two years or to both.
fields.elements: ["[\"An act of melting down, breaking up, or defacing by stamping any name, word or mark\", \"The object of the act is coin current for the time being in Tanzania\", \"The act is committed by a person\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine of fifty thousand shillings or imprisonment for two years or both.

fields.title: Misappropriation by director or officer and omission to record (with intent to defraud)
fields.description: Being a director or officer of a corporation or company, receiving or possessing any property of the corporation or company otherwise than in payment of a just debt or demand, and, with intent to defraud, omitting either to make a full and true entry of that receipt in the books and accounts of the corporation or company or to cause or direct such an entry to be made, is an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor is a director or officer of a corporation or company\", \"Actor receives or possesses property of the corporation or company\", \"The receipt/possession is otherwise than in payment of a just debt or demand\", \"Actor omits to make a full and true entry of the receipt/possession in the books and accounts of the corporation or company or omits to cause such an entry to be made\", \"The omission is done with intent to defraud\"]"]

fields.title: Misprision of treason
fields.description: A person commits misprision of treason if they (a) become an accessory after the fact to treason or to a treasonable offence; or (b) knowing that any person intends to commit treason or a treasonable offence, fail to give information with all reasonable dispatch to an administrative officer, a magistrate or a police officer, or fail to use other reasonable endeavours to prevent the commission of the offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Prohibited conduct (alternative A): becomes an accessory after the fact to treason or to a treasonable offence\", \"Prohibited conduct (alternative B): knowing that any person intends to commit treason or a treasonable offence, fails to give information with all reasonable dispatch to an administrative officer, magistrate or police officer, or fails to use other reasonable endeavours to prevent the commission of the offence\", \"Subject matter: treason or a treasonable offence\"]"]

fields.title: Neglecting to provide food, etc., for children
fields.description: A person who, being the parent or guardian or other person having the lawful care or charge of any child whose apparent age is not more than eighteen years and unable to provide for itself, refuses or neglects to provide, being able to do so, sufficient food, clothes, bedding and other necessaries of life for the child, so as to injure the health of that child, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Accused is the parent, guardian or other person having the lawful care or charge of the child\", \"Child\u2019s apparent age is not more than eighteen years\", \"Child is unable to provide for itself\", \"Accused refuses or neglects to provide sufficient food, clothes, bedding or other necessaries of life\", \"Accused is able to provide those necessities\", \"Refusal or neglect is such as to injure the health of the child\"]"]

fields.title: Neglect of official duty
fields.description: A person employed in the public service who wilfully neglects to perform any duty which he is bound by common law or by any written law to perform commits an offence. The provision contains an exception relating to the danger attendant on discharging the duty.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person employed in the public service\", \"Existence of a duty the person is bound to perform by common law or by written law\", \"Wilful neglect to perform that duty\"]"]

fields.title: Neglect to prevent offence
fields.description: A person who, knowing that another person designs to commit or is committing an offence, fails to use all reasonable means to prevent the commission or completion of that offence, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"There is a person (the actor) who knows that another person designs to commit or is committing an offence\", \"The actor fails to use all reasonable means available to them to prevent the commission or completion of that offence\", \"The failure occurs while the other person is designing to commit or is committing the offence\"]"]

fields.title: Negligent act likely to spread infection
fields.description: A person who unlawfully or negligently does any act which is, and which he knows or has reason to believe to be, likely to spread the infection of any disease dangerous to life commits an offence. The provision criminalises performing such acts with knowledge or a reasonable belief that they are likely to spread a life‑dangerous disease.
fields.elements: ["[\"actor is a person\", \"the person does an act\", \"the act is done unlawfully or negligently\", \"the act is one that is likely to spread infection\", \"the disease to be spread is dangerous to life\", \"the person knows or has reason to believe the act is likely to spread the infection\"]"]

fields.title: Negligently or unlawfully permitting escape of a prisoner of war
fields.description: A person who negligently or unlawfully permits the escape of any person referred to in paragraph (a) (an enemy of the United Republic who is a prisoner of war, whether confined or at large on parole) commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The defendant permitted another person to escape\", \"The person who escaped was an enemy of the United Republic who was a prisoner of war (as described in paragraph (a))\", \"The permitting was negligent or unlawful\"]"]

fields.title: Obliterating, adding to or altering the crossing on a cheque
fields.description: A person who, with intent to defraud, obliterates, adds to or alters the crossing on a cheque commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person\", \"There is a cheque with a crossing\", \"The accused obliterates, adds to or alters the crossing on the cheque\", \"The act is done with intent to defraud\"]"]

fields.title: Obtaining credit, etc., by false pretences
fields.description: A person who, by false pretences or other fraud, with intent to defraud, obtains credits in an account or causes an account to be credited; or who obtains credit in incurring a debt by false pretence or other fraud; or who, with intent to defraud creditors, makes or causes to be made any gift, delivery, transfer or charge of his property; or who, with intent to defraud creditors, conceals, sells or removes any part of his property after or within two months before an unsatisfied judgment or order for payment obtained against him, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person (actor)\", \"An act consisting of one of the prohibited behaviours listed in (a)\u2013(d)\", \"Use of false pretences or any other means of fraud (where specified)\", \"Intent to defraud (expressly required in (a), (c) and (d); fraud/false pretence implies dishonest conduct)\"]"]

fields.title: Obtaining execution of a security by false pretences
fields.description: A person who, by any false pretence and with intent to defraud, induces any person to execute, make, accept, endorse, alter or destroy the whole or part of any valuable security, or to write any name or impress or affix any seal upon any paper or parchment so that it may afterwards be made, converted into, used or dealt with as a valuable security, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused used a false pretence to induce another person\", \"The accused acted with intent to defraud\", \"The accused induced the other person to do one or more of the following in relation to a valuable security: execute, make, accept, endorse, alter or destroy the whole or part of it\", \"Or the accused induced the other person to write any name or impress or affix any seal upon any paper or parchment so that it may afterwards be made, converted into, used or dealt with as a valuable security\"]"]

fields.title: Obtaining goods by false pretences
fields.description: A person who, by any false pretence and with intent to defraud, obtains from any other person anything capable of being stolen or induces any other person to deliver to any person anything capable of being stolen, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"By any false pretence\", \"With intent to defraud\", \"Obtains from any other person anything capable of being stolen\", \"Or induces any other person to deliver to any person anything capable of being stolen\"]"]

fields.title: Obtaining registration, etc., by false pretence
fields.description: A person who wilfully procures or attempts to procure for himself or any other person any registration, licence or certificate under any law by any false pretence commits an offence. The offence attracts a maximum penalty of imprisonment for two years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Wilfully procures or attempts to procure\", \"The thing procured is a registration, licence or certificate\", \"Procurement is for the person themself or for any other person\", \"Procurement is by any false pretence\", \"The registration, licence or certificate is under any law\"]"]

fields.title: Offensive trades
fields.description: A person who, for the purposes of trade or otherwise, makes loud noises or offensive unwholesome smells in places and circumstances that annoy a considerable number of persons in the exercise of their common rights commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused made loud noises or caused offensive/unwholesome smells\", \"The conduct was done for the purposes of trade or otherwise\", \"The conduct occurred in places and circumstances such that it annoyed any considerable number of persons\", \"The persons annoyed were in the exercise of their common rights\"]"]
fields.penalty: Liable to the same penalty prescribed under section 171A(1).

fields.title: Offering indignity to a human corpse
fields.description: A person who, with the intention of wounding the feelings of any person or of insulting the religion of any person, or with the knowledge that such feelings or religion are likely to be insulted, offers any indignity to any human corpse commits an offence.
