fields.elements: ["[\"The accused offered an indignity to a human corpse\", \"The accused acted with either the intention of wounding the feelings of any person or of insulting the religion of any person, or with knowledge that the feelings of any person are likely to be wounded or that the religion of any person is likely to be insulted thereby\"]"]

fields.title: Officers charged with administration of property of special character with special duties
fields.description: A person employed in the public service who, by virtue of employment, is charged with judicial or administrative duties respecting property (or the carrying on of a manufacture, trade or business) of a special character, and who has acquired or holds, directly or indirectly, a private interest in that property, manufacture, trade or business, and discharges those duties in relation to the interest or the conduct of any person in relation thereto, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person is employed in the public service\", \"Person is charged by virtue of employment with judicial or administrative duties respecting property of a special character or respecting the carrying on of any manufacture, trade or business of a special character\", \"Person has acquired or holds, directly or indirectly, a private interest in such property, manufacture, trade or business\", \"Person discharges any such duties with respect to the property, manufacture, trade or business in which he has such interest or with respect to the conduct of any person in relation thereto\"]"]

fields.title: Other conspiracies
fields.description: A person who conspires with another to effect any of the listed purposes (paras (a)–(g)) commits an offence. The listed purposes include preventing enforcement of written law, causing injury to persons or property, obstructing disposition or exercise of trade, effecting unlawful purposes, or effecting lawful purposes by unlawful means.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person conspires with another (an agreement/ common design to act together)\", \"The object of the conspiracy is to effect one or more of the purposes described in subsection (1)(a)\u2013(g)\"]"]

fields.title: Other negligent acts causing harm
fields.description: A person who unlawfully does any act or omits to do any act which it is his duty to do, not being an act or omission specified in section 233, and by which harm is caused to any person, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused unlawfully does an act or omits to do an act\", \"The omission is of an act which it is the accused's duty to do\", \"The act or omission causes harm to any person\", \"The act or omission is not one specified in section 233\"]"]

fields.title: Other unlawful oaths to commit offences
fields.description: A person who administers, or is present at and consents to the administering of, any oath or engagement purporting to bind the taker to specified unlawful acts, or who (voluntarily) takes any such oath or engagement, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person administers, or is present at and consents to the administering of, an oath or engagement in the nature of an oath purporting to bind the person who takes it; or\", \"A person takes such an oath or engagement, provided the taker is not compelled to do so; and\", \"The oath purports to bind the taker to one of the specified categories of conduct (see alternatives)\"]"]

fields.title: Participation in gang robbery
fields.description: Makes it an offence for a person to take part in gang robbery. On conviction, the offender is liable to imprisonment for a term of not less than thirty years with or without corporal punishment.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person\", \"The accused took part in a robbery carried out by a gang (i.e. gang robbery)\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for a term of not less than thirty years with or without corporal punishment.

fields.title: Perjury
fields.description: A person who commits perjury shall be liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person commits perjury (as prohibited by law)\"]"]

fields.title: Permitting, managing or assisting in a common betting house
fields.description: A person who, being the owner or occupier of any house, room or place, knowingly and wilfully permits it to be opened, kept or used as a common betting house by another person, or who has the use or management of, or assists in conducting the business of, a common betting house, commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for one year; it is not an offence to use a totalisator by certain recognised clubs at a public meeting with the approval of the Inspector‑General of Police (see proviso).
fields.elements: ["[\"Person is the owner or occupier of any house, room or place (for the permitting limb)\", \"Knowingly and wilfully permits the place to be opened, kept or used as a common betting house by another person\", \"Or has the use or management of a common betting house\", \"Or assists in conducting the business of a common betting house\"]"]

fields.title: Personating public officers
fields.description: A person commits an offence who (a) personates any person employed in the public service on an occasion when that person is required to do any act or attend in any place by virtue of his employment; or (b) falsely represents himself to be a person employed in the public service and assumes to do any act or to attend in any place for the purpose of doing any act by virtue of such employment.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused person personated or falsely represented himself to be a person employed in the public service\", \"The impersonation or representation related to assuming to do an act or to attend in a place by virtue of the employment (or occurred on an occasion when the person impersonated was required to do an act or attend a place by virtue of employment)\"]"]

fields.title: Personation in general
fields.description: A person who, with intent to defraud any person, falsely represents himself to be some other person, living or dead, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused falsely represents himself to be some other person\", \"The representation is made to some other person (a target of the representation)\", \"The accused acts with intent to defraud\"]"]

fields.title: Personation of person named in certificate
fields.description: A person who utters a document issued by lawful authority to another person that certifies that other person as possessing a recognised qualification, holding an office, being entitled to exercise a profession/trade/business, or entitled to any right/privilege/rank/status, and who falsely represents himself to be the person named in the document, commits an offence and is liable to the same punishment as if he had forged the document.
fields.elements: ["[\"Utters (uses/presents) a document that was issued by lawful authority to another person\", \"The document certifies that the other person has a qualification recognised by law, or is holder of an office, or is entitled to exercise a profession, trade or business, or is entitled to any right, privilege, rank or status\", \"The defendant falsely represents himself to be the person named in the document\"]"]
fields.penalty: Liable to the same punishment as if he had forged the document.

fields.title: Personation of person named in testimonial of character
fields.description: A person who, for the purpose of obtaining any employment, utters any document of the nature of a testimonial character given to another person commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for one year.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Act: utters (uses or presents) a document\", \"Object: the document is of the nature of a testimonial character given to another person\", \"Purpose: for the purpose of obtaining any employment\"]"]

fields.title: Personation to obtain property by false claim of entitlement
fields.description: Where the representation is that the offender is a person entitled by will or operation of law to any specific property, and he commits the offence to obtain that property or possession thereof, he is liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused falsely represents himself to be a person entitled to a specific property\", \"The entitlement asserted is by will or by operation of law\", \"The false representation is made for the purpose of obtaining the specific property or possession of it\"]"]

fields.title: Piracy
fields.description: A person who (a) commits an act of violence, detention or degradation for private ends by the crew or passengers of a private ship or private aircraft directed against another ship or aircraft, or against persons or property on board such ship or aircraft or in a place outside the jurisdiction of any state; (b) participates in the operation of a ship or aircraft knowing facts that make it a pirate ship or aircraft; or (c) incites or intentionally facilitates such acts, commits an act of piracy. A person who does or participates in piracy is guilty of an offence and on conviction is liable to imprisonment for life.
fields.elements: ["[\"a person (any natural person)\", \"one or more prohibited acts: act of violence, detention or act of degradation; participation in operation of a ship/aircraft; or inciting/ intentionally facilitating such acts\", \"act directed against a ship or aircraft, or against persons or property on board such ship or aircraft, or against ship/aircraft/persons/property in a place outside the jurisdiction of any state\", \"conduct occurs in the context of a private ship or private aircraft, or a pirate ship or aircraft (as defined)\"]"]

fields.title: Playing a game of chance for money or money's worth in a public place
fields.description: A person who plays at any game of chance for money or money's worth in a public place shall be deemed an idle and disorderly person and liable to the prescribed penalty.
fields.elements: ["[\"The person plays at a game of chance\", \"The game is played for money or for money's worth\", \"The game is played in a public place\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a period not exceeding three months or to both.

fields.title: Possession, conveying or control of goods reasonably suspected to be stolen or unlawfully acquired
fields.description: A person who (a) having been detained under section 26 of the Criminal Procedure Act is found in possession of or conveying anything reasonably suspected of having been stolen or otherwise unlawfully acquired, or (b) is found by a police officer in possession of or having control over property which may, having regard to all the circumstances, be reasonably suspected of having been stolen or otherwise unlawfully acquired, may be charged; and if he fails to satisfy the court that he did not steal or otherwise unlawfully acquire the property he commits the offence and is liable on conviction to imprisonment for a term not exceeding three years.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person is found in one of the described circumstances\", \"Either (a) the person has been detained under section 26 of the Criminal Procedure Act and is found in possession of or conveying anything reasonably suspected of being stolen or unlawfully acquired,\", \"Or (b) a police officer finds the person in possession of or having control over property which may, having regard to all the circumstances, be reasonably suspected of being stolen or unlawfully acquired,\", \"The person fails to satisfy the court that he did not steal or otherwise unlawfully acquire the property\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for a term not exceeding three years.

fields.title: Possession of clippings
fields.description: A person who unlawfully has in his possession, or disposes of, any filings or clippings of gold or silver, or any gold or silver in bullion, dust, solution or other state, obtained by dealing with gold or silver coin in such a manner as to diminish its weight, knowing them to have been so obtained, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Unlawfully has in his possession, or disposes of\", \"Filings or clippings of gold or silver, or any gold or silver in bullion, dust, solution or any other state\", \"That were obtained by dealing with gold or silver coin in such a manner as to diminish its weight\", \"Knowledge that the material was so obtained\"]"]

fields.title: Possession of fraudulently altered stamps or stamped material without lawful excuse
fields.description: A person who knowingly and without lawful excuse (proof whereof lies on him) has in his possession any stamp or part of a stamp which has been fraudulently cut, torn or otherwise removed from any material, or any stamp which has been fraudulently mutilated, or any stamped material out of which any name, sum, date or other matter has been fraudulently erased or removed is liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"has in his possession any stamp or part of a stamp which has been fraudulently cut, torn or otherwise removed from any material; or\", \"has in his possession any stamp which has been fraudulently mutilated; or\", \"has in his possession any stamped material out of which any name, sum, date or other matter has been fraudulently erased or otherwise removed\"]"]

fields.title: Possession of human being parts
fields.description: A person who is found in unlawful possession of human being parts commits an offence. On conviction, the person is liable to imprisonment for a period not exceeding thirty years.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"is in possession of human being parts\", \"the possession is unlawful\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for a period not exceeding thirty years.

fields.title: Possession or disposal of items obtained or prepared by unlawful acts under this section
fields.description: A person who knowingly has in his possession or disposes of anything obtained or prepared by any unlawful act prohibited under paragraphs (a) to (f) commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person has possession of or disposes of an item\", \"Item was obtained or prepared by an unlawful act falling under paragraphs (a)\u2013(f)\", \"Possession or disposal is knowing\", \"No lawful authority or excuse (proof on the accused)\"]"]

fields.title: Possession or disposal of paper or material bearing impressions or stamp-related marks
fields.description: A person who knowingly has in his possession or disposes of any paper or other material bearing the impression of any die, plate or instrument, or bearing or containing words, figures, letters, marks or lines referred to in paragraph (a), without lawful authority or excuse, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person has possession of or disposes of paper or other material\", \"Material has an impression of a die, plate or instrument, or has words/figures/letters/marks/lines as described in paragraph (a)\", \"Possession or disposal is knowing\", \"No lawful authority or excuse (proof on the accused)\"]"]

fields.title: Preparations for coining
fields.description: A person who (a) gilds or silvers, or (b) makes any piece of metal into, a fit size or figure to be coined with intent that it shall be coined into counterfeit coin; or (c) without lawful authority or excuse (proof of which lies on him) engages in specified dealings with counterfeit coin, brings counterfeit coin into Tanzania knowing it to be counterfeit, or makes/has/disposes of stamps, moulds, presses, tools or machines adapted for making or assisting the making of (counterfeit) coin, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Gilding or silvering any piece of metal of a fit size or figure to be coined\", \"Making any piece of metal into a fit size or figure to be coined\", \"Buying, selling, receiving, paying or disposing of counterfeit coin at a lower rate than it imports or is apparently intended to import (or offering to do so)\", \"Bringing or receiving into Tanzania any counterfeit coin, knowing it to be counterfeit\", \"Making, mending, beginning or preparing to make or mend, possessing or disposing of any stamp or mould adapted to make the resemblance of either side (or part) of a coin, knowing it to be so adapted\", \"Making, mending, beginning or preparing to make or mend, possessing or disposing of any tool, instrument or machine adapted and intended to be used for marking coin edges to resemble coin edges, knowing it to be so adapted and intended\", \"Making, mending, beginning or preparing to make or mend, possessing or disposing of any coinage press or any tool/instrument/machine adapted for cutting round blanks from metal, knowing it to have been used or intended to be used for making counterfeit coin\"]"]

fields.title: Pretending to tell fortunes
fields.description: A person who, for gain or reward, undertakes to tell fortunes or who, by claiming skill or knowledge in any occult science, pretends to discover where or in what manner anything supposed to have been stolen or lost, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor is a person who undertakes to tell fortunes OR pretends (by reference to skill or knowledge in any occult science) to discover where or in what manner something supposed to have been stolen or lost\", \"The undertaking or pretence is made for gain or reward\"]"]

fields.title: Preventing escape from wreck
fields.description: A person who unlawfully prevents or obstructs any person who is on board of, or is escaping from, a vessel in distress or wrecked, in his endeavours to save his life, or unlawfully obstructs any person in his endeavour to save the life of any person in those circumstances, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused prevented or obstructed another person\", \"The other person was on board of, or escaping from, a vessel which was in distress or wrecked and was endeavouring to save his life, or the other person was endeavouring to save the life of any person in those circumstances\", \"The prevention or obstruction was unlawful\"]"]

fields.title: Preventing or obstructing making of proclamation
fields.description: A person who forcibly prevents or obstructs the making of a proclamation referred to in section 77 commits an offence. The offence attracts a maximum penalty of imprisonment for ten years.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"forcibly prevents or obstructs the making of a proclamation referred to in section 77\"]"]

fields.title: Preventing or obstructing service or execution of process
fields.description: A person who wilfully obstructs or knowingly prevents, interferes with, or resists the service upon any person of a summons, notice, order, warrant or other process issued by a court; wilfully obstructs or knowingly prevents, interferes with, or resists the execution of any such process or of any person lawfully charged with its execution; or absconds in order to avoid being served with any such process, commits an offence. On conviction the person is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding one year.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Prohibited conduct: wilfully obstructs, knowingly prevents, interferes with, or resists; or absconds\", \"Object: service or execution of a summons, notice, order, warrant or other process\", \"Source of process: process issued by a court\", \"Target of service/execution: service upon himself or any other person, or execution by a person lawfully charged with it\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for a term not exceeding one year.

fields.title: Procuration for prostitution
fields.description: A person who, by procuring, attempting to procure, bringing, removing, detaining or attempting such acts in relation to another person, causes or intends that that person become a prostitute, be taken to or frequent a brothel, or be made subject to prohibited sexual intercourse or sexual abuse, commits the offence of procuration. The provision covers acts done within or outside the United Republic and applies whether or not the person consents.
fields.elements: ["[\"Accused is a person who procures, attempts to procure, brings, removes, detains, or attempts any of those acts in relation to another person\", \"The act is done with the purpose or view that the other person becomes a prostitute, facilitates prohibited sexual intercourse, becomes an inmate of or frequents a brothel, or is subject to prohibited sexual intercourse or sexual abuse\", \"Acts may occur within or outside the United Republic (including causing a person to leave or be brought into the United Republic)\", \"Consent of the person is immaterial in several alternatives (the provision expressly states 'whether with or without the consent')\", \"For detention offence (paragraph (f)), the person is detained without that person's consent\"]"]
fields.penalty: On conviction, liable to a fine of not less than one hundred thousand shillings and not exceeding three hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a term of not less than ten years and not exceeding twenty years or to both.

fields.title: Procuring an offence
fields.description: A person who procures another to do or omit to do any act which, if he had himself done the act or made the omission, would have constituted an offence, is guilty of an offence of the same kind and is liable to the same punishment as if he had himself done the act or omission.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person procures another person to do or omit an act\", \"The act or omission is such that, if the procurer had done it or omitted it, it would have constituted an offence\"]"]
fields.penalty: Liable to the same punishment as if he had himself done the act or omission.

fields.title: Procuring execution of deeds, etc., by threats
fields.description: A person who, with intent to defraud, by unlawful violence or restraint, or by threats (including accusing or threatening to accuse of an offence, or offering/making a solicitation or threat as an inducement to commit or permit an offence), compels or induces another person to execute, make, accept, endorse, alter or destroy any part or whole of a valuable security, or to write any name or affix any seal on paper or parchment so it may be made, converted into, used or dealt with as a valuable security, commits an offence. The offender is liable to imprisonment for fourteen years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Defendant compelled or induced another person\", \"Means: unlawful violence to or restraint of the person, or threat of violence or restraint to be used on the person, or accusing/threatening to accuse any person of an offence, or offering/making any solicitation or threat as an inducement to commit or permit the commission of an offence\", \"Target conduct: (i) execute, make, accept, endorse, alter or destroy the whole or any part of any valuable security; or (ii) write any name or impress or affix any seal upon any paper or parchment so it may be afterwards made, converted into, used or dealt with as a valuable security\", \"With intent to defraud\"]"]

fields.title: Procuring execution of documents by false pretences
fields.description: A person who, by means of any false and fraudulent representations as to the nature, contents or operation of a document, procures another person to sign or execute the document commits an offence and is liable to the same punishment as if he had forged the document.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person who makes representations\", \"Making false and fraudulent representations as to the nature, contents or operation of a document\", \"Procures another person to sign or execute the document\"]"]
fields.penalty: Shall be liable to the same punishment, as if he had forged the document.

fields.title: Procuring married woman under eighteen for intercourse with husband
fields.description: A person who procures or attempts to procure any married woman under the age of eighteen years with intent that she shall have sexual intercourse with her husband, whether with or without her consent, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Accused procures or attempts to procure the woman\", \"The woman is married\", \"The woman is under the age of eighteen years\", \"Accused intends that she shall have sexual intercourse with her husband\"]"]

fields.title: Procuring own miscarriage
fields.description: A woman who is with child who, with intent to procure her own miscarriage, unlawfully administers to herself any poison or other noxious thing, uses any force, uses any other means, or permits any such thing or means to be administered or applied to her, commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a woman being with child (pregnant)\", \"The accused acts with intent to procure her own miscarriage\", \"The accused unlawfully administers to herself any poison or other noxious thing, or uses any force of any kind, or uses any other means whatsoever, or permits any such thing or means to be administered or applied to her\"]"]

fields.title: Procuring rape
fields.description: A person who, by threats or intimidation, by false pretences or false representations, or by applying or administering any drug or similar thing with intent to stupefy or overpower, procures or attempts to procure any girl or woman to have prohibited sexual intercourse inside or outside the United Republic commits an offence. On conviction the person is liable to a fine of not less than one hundred thousand shillings and not exceeding three hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for a term of not less than ten years and not exceeding twenty years or to both, and shall be ordered to pay compensation determined by the court.
fields.elements: ["[\"the accused is a person\", \"the accused procures or attempts to procure any girl or woman to have prohibited sexual intercourse\", \"the procurement is effected by means specified in the alternatives\", \"the prohibited sexual intercourse may occur inside or outside the United Republic\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine of not less than one hundred thousand shillings and not exceeding three hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for a term of not less than ten years and not exceeding twenty years or to both.

fields.title: Production, making or possession of obscene matter for trade, distribution or public exhibition
fields.description: A person who, for the purpose of or by way of trade or for the purpose of distribution or public exhibition, makes, produces or has in his possession any obscene writing, drawing, print, painting, printed matter, picture, poster, emblem, photograph, cinematograph film or any other obscene object or other object tending to corrupt morals commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Makes, produces or has in possession any obscene writing, drawing, print, painting, printed matter, picture, poster, emblem, photograph, cinematograph film or any other obscene object or other object tending to corrupt morals\", \"Act is for the purpose of or by way of trade or for the purpose of distribution or public exhibition\"]"]
fields.penalty: Fine of two hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for two years.

fields.title: Prohibition against inducement
fields.description: A person who induces or attempts to induce any person to (a) make any payment to or for the benefit of the promoter or any participant in a scheme; or (b) become a participant and to make any payment to or for the benefit of the promoter or any participant in a scheme, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person who induces or attempts to induce another person\", \"Inducement to make any payment to or for the benefit of the promoter or any participant in a scheme\", \"Or inducement to become a participant and to make any payment to or for the benefit of the promoter or any participant in a scheme\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine not exceeding five million shillings or imprisonment for a term not exceeding five years.

fields.title: Promoting warlike undertakings
fields.description: A person who, without lawful authority, carries on or makes preparation for carrying on, or aids in or devises the carrying on of, or preparation for, any war or warlike undertaking with, for, by or against any person or body or group of persons in the United Republic commits an offence. On conviction the offender is liable to imprisonment for life.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Absence of lawful authority to undertake the conduct\", \"Prohibited target conduct: carrying on any war or warlike undertaking\", \"Prohibited preparatory conduct: making preparation for carrying on any war or warlike undertaking\", \"Prohibited assisting conduct: aiding in the carrying on of, or preparation for, any war or warlike undertaking\", \"Prohibited planning conduct: devising the carrying on of, or preparation for, any war or warlike undertaking\", \"Relation to others: the war or warlike undertaking is with, for, by or against any person, body or group of persons in the United Republic\"]"]

fields.title: Public conduct likely to cause a breach of the peace
fields.description: A person who publicly conducts himself in a manner likely to cause a breach of the peace shall be deemed an idle and disorderly person and liable to the prescribed penalty.
fields.elements: ["[\"The person conducts himself publicly\", \"The conduct is of a kind likely to cause a breach of the peace\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a period not exceeding three months or to both.

fields.title: Public exhibition of indecent or morals-corrupting shows or performances
fields.description: A person who publicly exhibits any indecent show or performance or any show or performance tending to corrupt morals commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Publicly exhibits any indecent show or performance or any show or performance tending to corrupt morals\", \"Exhibition is public\"]"]
fields.penalty: Fine of two hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for two years.

fields.title: Public indecent act without lawful excuse
fields.description: A person who, without lawful excuse, publicly does any indecent act shall be deemed an idle and disorderly person and liable to the prescribed penalty.
fields.elements: ["[\"The person does an indecent act\", \"The act is done publicly\", \"There is no lawful excuse for the act\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a period not exceeding three months or to both.

fields.title: Punishment of accessories after fact to offences punishable with less than three years imprisonment
fields.description: A person who becomes an accessory after the fact to an offence that is punishable with imprisonment for less than three years commits an offence under this section.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person (actor)\", \"The person becomes an accessory after the fact to an offence\", \"The offence to which the person is accessory is punishable with imprisonment for less than three years\"]"]

fields.title: Punishment of attempts to commit offence
fields.description: A person who attempts to commit an offence which, if convicted, would be punishable by death or by imprisonment for a term of fourteen years or more commits an offence. Where no other punishment is provided, the person shall be liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person (actor) undertakes an attempt to commit an offence\", \"The offence attempted is of such a kind that conviction for it would attract punishment of death or imprisonment for a term of fourteen years or more\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for seven years (where no other punishment is provided).

fields.title: Purchasing forged notes
fields.description: A person who, without lawful authority or excuse (the proof of which lies on him), purchases or receives from any person, or has in his possession, a forged bank note or currency note, whether filled up or in blank, knowing it to be forged, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person\", \"The accused purchases a forged bank note or currency note; or receives such a note from any person; or has such a note in his possession\", \"The note is a forged bank note or currency note (whether filled up or in blank)\", \"The accused knows the note to be forged\"]"]

fields.title: Raising discontent and ill-will for unlawful purposes
fields.description: A person who, to any assembly, makes any statement likely to raise discontent among the inhabitants of the United Republic or to promote feelings of ill-will between different classes or communities of persons of the United Republic commits an offence. The offender is liable to a fine not less than five hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a term of not less than one year.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Makes a statement to an assembly\", \"The statement is likely to raise discontent among inhabitants of the United Republic or to promote feelings of ill-will between different classes or communities of persons of the United Republic\", \"No other statutory exception applies\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine not less than five hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for a term of not less than one year.

fields.title: Rape
fields.description: A person who commits rape is liable to imprisonment for life, and in any case to imprisonment of not less than thirty years with corporal punishment and a fine, and shall be ordered to pay compensation determined by the court to the person in respect of whom the offence was committed. Special sentencing rules apply where the offender is a boy below eighteen years and where the victim is a girl under ten years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The person commits the offence of rape (as defined in the law; see the substantive definition elsewhere)\", \"Conviction for that offence\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for life, and in any case imprisonment of not less than thirty years with corporal punishment, and a fine, and ordered to pay compensation determined by the court.

fields.title: Rape
fields.description: It is an offence for a male person to rape a girl or a woman. A male person commits rape if he has sexual intercourse (penetration however slight) with a girl or woman under any of the circumstances described in the section (non‑consensual intercourse; consent obtained by force, threats, intimidation or while unlawfully detained; consent vitiated by unsoundness of mind or intoxication induced by the man or another person; consent induced by deception about identity; intercourse with a person under 18 except in a limited spousal exception), or if he takes advantage of certain positions of authority to commit rape.
fields.elements: ["[\"male person\", \"sexual intercourse with a girl or a woman (penetration however slight)\", \"absence of lawful consent or consent vitiated by force, threats, intimidation, unlawful detention, unsoundness of mind, intoxication induced by the man or another, or deception as to identity\", \"or intercourse with a person under 18 years of age (subject to the statutory spousal exception)\", \"or committing intercourse by taking advantage of an official/managerial/duty\u2011of\u2011care/traditional healer/religious position or by wrongful restraint\"]"]

fields.title: Receiving goods stolen from outside Mainland Tanzania
fields.description: A person who, without lawful excuse, knowing that anything has been stolen or obtained in such circumstances that if the act had been committed in Mainland Tanzania the person committing it would have been guilty of an offence, receives or has in his possession anything so stolen or obtained outside Mainland Tanzania commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person receives or has in his possession anything stolen or obtained outside Mainland Tanzania\", \"The property was stolen or obtained outside Mainland Tanzania\", \"The person knew the property had been stolen or obtained in circumstances which would have been an offence if committed in Mainland Tanzania\", \"Act is done without lawful excuse\"]"]

fields.title: Receiving property stolen or unlawfully obtained, etc
fields.description: A person who receives or retains any chattel, money, valuable security or other property, knowing or having reason to believe it to have been stolen, extorted, wrongfully or unlawfully taken, obtained, converted or disposed of, commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for ten years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused receives or retains property\", \"The property is any chattel, money, valuable security or other property\", \"The property was stolen, extorted, wrongfully or unlawfully taken, obtained, converted or disposed of\", \"At the time of receipt or retention, the accused knew or had reason to believe the property was so obtained\"]"]

fields.title: Reckless and negligent acts
fields.description: A person who, in a manner so rash or negligent as to endanger human life or to be likely to cause harm to any other person, commits an offence if they perform any of the listed acts or omissions (a)–(h). The provision covers various acts and omissions such as driving, navigating, handling fire, animals, machinery, explosives, providing medical treatment, or supplying dangerous substances when done so rashly or negligently.
fields.elements: ["[\"Accused is a person\", \"Accused acts in a manner so rash or negligent as to endanger human life or be likely to cause harm to any other person\", \"Accused does one of the prohibited acts or omissions set out in paragraphs (a)\u2013(h)\"]"]

fields.title: Removal, etc., of property under lawful seizure
fields.description: A person who, when any property has been attached or taken under process of any court, knowingly and with intent to hinder or defeat the attachment or process, receives, removes, retains, conceals or disposes of the property commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Property has been attached or taken under the process or authority of a court\", \"The accused receives, removes, retains, conceals or disposes of that property\", \"The act is done knowingly\", \"The act is done with intent to hinder or defeat the attachment or process\"]"]

fields.title: Removing boundary marks with intent to defraud
fields.description: A person who wilfully and unlawfully, and with intent to defraud, removes or defaces any object or mark lawfully erected or made as an indication of the boundary of any land commits an offence. The offence attracts a maximum term of imprisonment of three years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person\", \"The accused wilfully and unlawfully removes or defaces an object or mark\", \"The object or mark was lawfully erected or made as an indication of the boundary of any land\", \"The act is done with intent to defraud\"]"]

fields.title: Repeated uttering
fields.description: Creates an offence where a person utters a counterfeit coin knowing it to be counterfeit and (a) at the time has any other counterfeit coin in possession, or (b) utters another counterfeit coin on the same day or within the next ten days knowing it to be counterfeit, or (c) has in possession three or more counterfeit coins knowing them to be counterfeit and with intent to utter any of them. The offender is liable to imprisonment for three years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person utters a coin that is counterfeit\", \"Person knows the coin uttered is counterfeit\", \"One of the following repeating circumstances is present: possession of any other counterfeit coin at the time of uttering; uttering another counterfeit coin on the same day or within ten days; possession of three or more counterfeit coins knowing them counterfeit with intent to utter any of them\"]"]

fields.title: Rescue
fields.description: A person who by force rescues or attempts to rescue from lawful custody any other person commits an offence. The penalty depends on the status of the person rescued: (a) if the person is under sentence of death or imprisonment for life, or charged with an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life — imprisonment for life; (b) if the person is imprisoned on a charge or under sentence for any other offence — imprisonment for seven years; (c) in any other case, the person commits an offence (penalty not specified in this subsection).
fields.elements: ["[\"Use of force to rescue or attempt to rescue another person\", \"Rescue or attempted rescue is from lawful custody\"]"]
fields.penalty: Penalty varies by status of rescued person: imprisonment for life; imprisonment for seven years; or (where unspecified) penalty not stated in this subsection.

fields.title: Rioters damaging buildings, etc
fields.description: A person who, while riotously assembled together, unlawfully damages any of the things listed in section 81 commits an offence. Each person so offending is liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person\", \"The accused is riotously assembled together with others\", \"The accused unlawfully damages any of the things referred to in section 81\"]"]

fields.title: Rioters demolishing buildings, etc
fields.description: A person who, being riotously assembled together, unlawfully pulls down or destroys, or begins to pull down or destroy, any building, railway, machinery or structures commits an offence. Each member of the riotous assembly is liable to imprisonment for life.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person who is riotously assembled together with others\", \"The accused unlawfully pulls down or destroys, or begins to pull down or destroy\", \"Target of the act is any building, railway, machinery or structures\", \"Liability attaches to each member of the riotous assembly who commits the act\"]"]

fields.title: Rioting after proclamation
fields.description: Where a proclamation is made commanding persons engaged in a riot or assembled with the purpose of committing a riot to disperse, any person who, at or after the expiration of a reasonable time from the making of the proclamation, takes or continues to take part in the riot or assembly commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for five years.
fields.elements: ["[\"A proclamation was made commanding persons engaged in a riot or assembled with the purpose of committing a riot to disperse\", \"A reasonable time has expired from the making of the proclamation\", \"The accused takes part in the riot or assembly or continues to take part in it\"]"]

fields.title: Robbery with violence
fields.description: A person who steals anything and, at or immediately before or immediately after the time of stealing it, uses or threatens to use actual violence to any person or property in order to obtain or retain the thing stolen or to prevent or overcome resistance to its being stolen or retained, commits robbery.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused steals anything (theft occurs)\", \"At or immediately before or immediately after the time of stealing\", \"The accused uses or threatens to use actual violence\", \"The violence or threat is to any person or to property\", \"The use/threat is in order to obtain or retain the thing stolen or to prevent or overcome resistance to its being stolen or retained\"]"]

fields.title: Rogue and vagabond (being)
fields.description: Any person who falls within any of the specified categories (a)–(e) in this section shall be deemed a rogue and vagabond and guilty of an offence. The categories include prior convictions, fraudulent collection of alms, suspected thieves without visible means of subsistence, persons found in circumstances suggesting an illegal or disorderly purpose, and persons collecting money in a public place without required written consent.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person who falls within at least one of the statutory categories (a)\u2013(e)\", \"Category (a): a person convicted of an offence under section 176 after having been previously convicted as an idle and disorderly person\", \"Category (b): a person going about as a gatherer or collector of alms, or endeavouring to procure charitable contributions of any nature or kind, under any false or fraudulent pretence\", \"Category (c): a suspected or reputed thief who has no visible means of subsistence and cannot give a good account of himself\", \"Category (d): a person found in or upon or near premises, road, highway or public place at such time and under such circumstances as to lead to the conclusion that the person is there for an illegal or disorderly purpose\", \"Category (e): a person who, without prior written consent of the District Commissioner or (for an urban authority) the police officer in charge, collects or appeals for subscriptions of money in any public place\"]"]
fields.penalty: For the first offence: imprisonment for three months; for every subsequent offence: imprisonment for one year.

fields.title: Sale, offer or exposure for sale of adulterated drugs
fields.description: A person who, knowing any drug or medical preparation has been adulterated in such a manner as to lessen its efficacy, to change its operation, or to render it noxious, sells it or offers or exposes it for sale commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor is a person\", \"The article is a drug or medical preparation\", \"The drug/medical preparation has been adulterated (so as to lessen efficacy, change operation, or render it noxious)\", \"The person knows of the adulteration\", \"The person sells, offers or exposes the drug/medical preparation for sale\"]"]

fields.title: Sale of noxious food or drink
fields.description: A person who sells, or offers or exposes for sale, as food or drink, any article which has been rendered or has become noxious, or is in a state unfit for food or drink, knowing or having reason to believe that it is noxious as food or drink, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person sells, offers or exposes for sale (as food or drink) an article\", \"The article has been rendered or has become noxious, or is in a state unfit for food or drink\", \"The person knows or has reason to believe that the article is noxious as food or drink\"]"]

fields.title: Sending or signing false certificate of marriage to registrar
fields.description: A person who signs or transmits to a person authorised to register marriages a certificate of marriage, or any document purporting to be a certificate of marriage, which in any material particular is to his knowledge false, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused signs or transmits\", \"The act is directed to a person authorised by law to register marriages\", \"The document is a certificate of marriage or a document purporting to be a certificate of marriage\", \"The document is false in any material particular\"]"]

fields.title: Setting fire to crops and growing plants
fields.description: A person who wilfully and unlawfully sets fire to a crop of cultivated produce, any stack of cultivated vegetable produce or of mineral or vegetable fuel, a crop of hay or grass under cultivation, or any standing trees, saplings or shrubs under cultivation commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"wilfully and unlawfully sets fire to property\", \"the property is one of the specified items (cultivated crop; stack of cultivated vegetable produce or mineral/vegetable fuel; crop of hay or grass under cultivation; standing trees, saplings or shrubs under cultivation)\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine of not less than one million shillings but not exceeding five million shillings or to imprisonment for a term of not less than two years but not exceeding five years or to both.

fields.title: Setting fire to thing so situated that property referred to in section 319 is likely to catch fire
fields.description: A person who wilfully and unlawfully sets fire to anything which is so situated that anything referred to in section 319 is likely to catch fire from it commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"the accused is a person\", \"the accused sets fire to an object\", \"the object is so situated that anything referred to in section 319 is likely to catch fire from it\", \"the act is unlawful\"]"]

fields.title: Severing with intent to steal
fields.description: A person who makes anything movable with intent to steal commits an offence and is liable to the same punishment as if they had stolen the thing after it had become movable.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused made (rendered) a thing movable\", \"The thing rendered movable was 'anything' (no particular category limited)\", \"The accused acted with intent to steal the thing\"]"]
fields.penalty: Liable to the same punishment as if he had stolen the thing after it had become movable.

fields.title: Sexual assault
fields.description: A person who, with the intention to cause sexual annoyance to any person, utters any word or sound, makes any gesture, or exhibits any word or object intending that it be heard or seen by that other person, commits the offence of sexual assault. On conviction the offender is liable to a fine not exceeding three hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding five years or to both.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person utters a word or sound, makes a gesture, or exhibits a word or object\", \"The act is done with the intention to cause sexual annoyance to any person\", \"The word/sound/gesture/object is intended to be heard or seen by that other person\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine not exceeding three hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for a term not exceeding five years or both.

fields.title: Sexual exploitation of children
fields.description: Creates the offence where a person permits, procures, induces, exploits or uses threats, influence or payment to obtain a child for sexual intercourse, any form of sexual abuse, or an obscene or indecent exhibition or show. On conviction the offender is liable to imprisonment for a term of not less than fifteen years and not exceeding thirty years without option of fine.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Victim: a child\", \"Prohibited outcome/purpose: causing the child to be sexually abused, to participate in sexual activity, or to appear in an obscene or indecent exhibition or show\", \"Prohibited conduct: permitting the child to remain on premises for that purpose; acting as procurer of a child; inducing a person to be a client of a child by media/advertisement; taking advantage of influence/relationship to procure; threatening or using violence to procure; giving money, goods or other benefits to child or parents with intent to procure\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for a term of not less than fifteen years and not exceeding thirty years without option of fine.

fields.title: Sexual harassment
fields.description: A person who, with intention, assaults or by use of criminal force sexually harasses another person, or by words or actions causes sexual annoyance or harassment to another person, commits the offence of sexual harassment. On conviction the offender is liable to a fine not exceeding two hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding five years or to both, and may be ordered to pay compensation to the victim.
fields.elements: ["[\"Accused is a person who performed an act upon another person\", \"The act consisted of an assault or use of criminal force OR words or actions causing sexual annoyance or harassment to the other person\", \"The conduct was intentional (done with intention)\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine not exceeding two hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for a term not exceeding five years or both.

fields.title: Soliciting for immoral purposes in a public place
fields.description: A person who in any public place solicits for immoral purposes shall be deemed an idle and disorderly person and liable to the prescribed penalty.
fields.elements: ["[\"The person solicits\", \"Solicitation is for immoral purposes\", \"Solicitation occurs in a public place\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a period not exceeding three months or to both.

fields.title: Soliciting or inciting commission of offence
fields.description: A person who solicits or incites another to commit an offence commits an offence. The offence is complete regardless of whether the solicitation or incitement has any effect.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused solicits or incites another person to commit an offence\", \"The solicitation or incitement concerns the commission of an offence\"]"]

fields.title: Stealing by agents, etc
fields.description: Offence of stealing where the thing stolen is property or proceeds that were received by the offender in a fiduciary, entrusted, or agency capacity (for example, under a power of attorney, entrusted for safe custody or for application on behalf of another, received for or on account of another, or proceeds of securities received with a direction).
fields.elements: ["[\"The offender dishonestly appropriates or steals property or proceeds (i.e. commits stealing)\", \"The thing stolen is one of the specified categories of property or proceeds received/held by the offender in a special capacity\", \"At the time of receiving or holding the property, the offender had it by virtue of a power of attorney, was entrusted with it (alone or jointly) for safe custody or to apply/pay/deliver it or its proceeds, received it for or on account of another person, or received proceeds of valuable security or disposal under a direction that the proceeds be applied or paid to a specified person or purpose\"]"]

fields.title: Stupefying in order to commit offence
fields.description: A person who, with intent to commit or to facilitate the commission of an offence, or to facilitate the flight of an offender after the commission or attempted commission of an offence, administers or attempts to administer any stupefying or overpowering drug or thing to any person commits an offence. The offender is liable to imprisonment for life.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person (actor)\", \"Administers or attempts to administer any stupefying or overpowering drug or thing\", \"Administered to any person (victim)\", \"Act done with relevant intent (see mental elements)\"]"]

fields.title: Suborning perjury
fields.description: A person who suborns perjury shall be liable to imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person suborns perjury (causes or procures another to commit perjury)\"]"]

fields.title: Supplying drugs or instruments to procure abortion
fields.description: A person who unlawfully supplies to or procures for another any thing whatsoever, knowing that it is intended to be unlawfully used to procure the miscarriage of a woman (whether she is or is not with child) commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for three years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Supply to another, or procure for another, any thing whatsoever\", \"The item is intended to be used to procure the miscarriage of a woman\", \"The supply or procurement is unlawful\"]"]

fields.title: Taking or continuing to take part in riot or assembly knowing proclamation was prevented
fields.description: Where the making of the proclamation is prevented, every person who, knowing that it has been so prevented, takes or continues to take part in the riot or assembly commits an offence. The offence attracts a maximum penalty of imprisonment for five years.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person takes or continues to take part in the riot or assembly\", \"the taking or continuing occurs when the making of the proclamation has been prevented\", \"the person knows that the making of the proclamation has been prevented\"]"]

fields.title: Taking part in an unlawful assembly
fields.description: A person who takes part in an unlawful assembly commits an offence and is liable to imprisonment for one year.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Takes part in an assembly\", \"The assembly is unlawful (as defined elsewhere)\"]"]

fields.title: Taking part in a riot
fields.description: A person who takes part in a riot commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused took part in a riot\"]"]

fields.title: Taking photos, pictures, videos or images of corpses, dead persons, victims of crimes or gruesome incidents (prohibited)
fields.description: Prohibits, save as provided in subsection (2), taking photos, pictures, videos or images of corpses, dead persons, victims of crimes or gruesome incidents. Contravention of this prohibition is an offence punishable as provided in subsection (3).
fields.elements: ["[\"a person\", \"took photos, pictures, videos or images\", \"the images depicted corpses, dead persons, victims of crimes or gruesome incidents\", \"the taking was not covered by the exceptions in subsection (2)\"]"]
fields.penalty: On conviction, liable to a fine of not less than one million shillings or to imprisonment for a term of not less than one year or to both.

fields.title: Theft
fields.description: A person who steals anything capable of being stolen commits theft. The offender is liable to imprisonment for seven years, unless owing to the circumstances of the theft or the nature of the thing stolen some other punishment is provided.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Conduct: steals (appropriates) property\", \"Object: anything capable of being stolen\"]"]

fields.title: Theft
fields.description: A person who fraudulently and without claim of right takes anything capable of being stolen, or fraudulently converts to the use of any person other than the general or special owner, steals that thing. The section defines when taking or conversion is deemed fraudulent and clarifies related matters such as who is a "special owner" and the requirement that taking involves movement.
fields.elements: ["[\"the accused is a person\", \"the accused takes or converts a thing\", \"the thing is capable of being stolen\", \"the taking or conversion is done fraudulently\", \"the taking or conversion is without claim of right\", \"if conversion, it is to the use of a person other than the general or special owner\"]"]

fields.title: Threatening violence or discharging firearm with intent to intimidate, annoy or alarm
fields.description: A person who, with intent to intimidate or annoy any person, threatens to injure, assault, shoot at or kill any person or to burn, destroy or damage any property; or who, with intent to alarm any person, discharges a firearm or commits any other breach of the peace, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person who engages in the prohibited conduct\", \"Prohibited conduct (alternative sets): threatens specified harms to a person or property; or discharges a firearm or commits another breach of the peace\", \"The conduct is done with the requisite intent (to intimidate, annoy or alarm as specified)\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for one year; if the offence is committed at night the offender is liable to imprisonment for two years.

fields.title: Threat of injury to persons employed in public service
fields.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to a person employed in the public service, or to any person believed by the offender to be interested in that person, with the purpose of inducing that person to do or to forbear or delay any act connected with the exercise of the public functions of that person, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused held out a threat of injury\", \"The threat was directed at a person employed in the public service, or at a person in whom the accused believed that public servant to be interested\", \"The threat was made for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do, or to forbear or delay to do, any act\", \"The act (or forbearance/delay) was connected with the exercise of the public functions of that person\"]"]

fields.title: Threats to burn or destroy
fields.description: A person who, knowing the contents thereof, sends, delivers, utters or directly or indirectly causes to be received any letter or writing threatening to burn or destroy any house, barn or other building or any rick or stack of grain, hay or straw, or other agricultural produce, whether in or under any building or not, or any vessel, or to kill, maim or wound any cattle commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused sent, delivered, uttered or directly or indirectly caused to be received any letter or writing\", \"The accused knew the contents of that letter or writing\", \"The letter or writing contained a threat to burn or destroy any house, barn, other building, any rick or stack of grain, hay or straw or other agricultural produce (whether in or under any building or not), or any vessel, or to kill, maim or wound any cattle\"]"]

fields.title: Treasonable offences
fields.description: A person who, not being under allegiance to the United Republic, in the United Republic or elsewhere, with intent to help any enemy of the United Republic, does acts which would amount to the offence of treason under section 39 commits an offence. On conviction the offender shall be sentenced to death.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor is a person not under allegiance to the United Republic\", \"Actor is in the United Republic or elsewhere when acting\", \"Actor performs acts which would amount to the offence of treason as defined in section 39\", \"Actor acts with intent to help any enemy of the United Republic\"]"]
fields.penalty: On conviction, shall be sentenced to death.

fields.title: Treason — acts intended to help an enemy or likely to assist/undermine public order, Government, Defence or Police, or endanger life
fields.description: A person who, being under allegiance to the United Republic, in the United Republic or elsewhere, with intent to help any enemy of the United Republic does any act designed or likely to give assistance to such enemy, or to interfere with the maintenance of public order or the Government, or to impede the operation of the Defence Forces or the Police Force, or to endanger life, commits treason.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person who is under allegiance to the United Republic\", \"Act occurs in the United Republic or elsewhere\", \"With intent to help any enemy of the United Republic, does an act designed or likely to give assistance to such enemy or to interfere with public order or the Government, or to impede the Defence Forces or Police Force, or to endanger life\"]"]
fields.penalty: On conviction, shall be sentenced to death.

fields.title: Treason — adhering to or giving aid or comfort to the enemies of the United Republic
fields.description: A person who, being under allegiance to the United Republic, adheres to the enemies of the United Republic or gives them aid or comfort, in the United Republic or elsewhere, commits treason.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person who is under allegiance to the United Republic\", \"Act occurs in the United Republic or elsewhere\", \"Adheres to enemies of the United Republic or gives them aid or comfort\"]"]
fields.penalty: On conviction, shall be sentenced to death.

fields.title: Treason — forming and manifesting intention to effect specified acts against the President or Government
fields.description: A person who, being under allegiance to the United Republic, forms an intention to effect or cause to be effected, or to instigate, persuade, counsel or advise others to effect specified acts (a)–(d), and manifests that intention by publishing any writing or printing or by any overt act or deed, commits treason.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person who is under allegiance to the United Republic\", \"Forms an intention to effect, to cause to be effected, or to instigate/persuade/counsel/advise others to effect one of the listed acts\", \"Manifests that intention by publishing any writing or printing or by any overt act or deed\"]"]
fields.penalty: On conviction, shall be sentenced to death.

fields.title: Treason — instigating invasion of the United Republic with an armed force
fields.description: A person who, being under allegiance to the United Republic, instigates, whether in the United Republic or elsewhere, any person to invade the United Republic with an armed force commits treason.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person who is under allegiance to the United Republic\", \"Act may occur in the United Republic or elsewhere\", \"Instigates any person to invade the United Republic with an armed force\"]"]
fields.penalty: On conviction, shall be sentenced to death.

fields.title: Treason — levying war against the United Republic
fields.description: A person who, being under allegiance to the United Republic, in the United Republic, levies war against the United Republic commits treason.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person who is under allegiance to the United Republic\", \"Act occurs in the United Republic\", \"Levying war against the United Republic\"]"]
fields.penalty: On conviction, shall be sentenced to death.

fields.title: Treason — murder or attempted murder of the President
fields.description: A person who, being under allegiance to the United Republic, in the United Republic or elsewhere, murders or attempts to murder the President commits treason.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person who is under allegiance to the United Republic\", \"Act occurs in the United Republic or elsewhere\", \"Murders the President or attempts to murder the President\"]"]
fields.penalty: On conviction, shall be sentenced to death.

fields.title: Treason — taking up arms within the United Republic to coerce, intimidate or overawe the Government
fields.description: A person who, being under allegiance to the United Republic, takes up arms within the United Republic in order, by force of constraint, to compel the Government to change its measures or counsels, or to put any force or constraint on, or to intimidate or overawe, the Government of the United Republic commits treason.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person who is under allegiance to the United Republic\", \"Act occurs within the United Republic\", \"Takes up arms\", \"Purpose is to compel the Government to change measures or counsels, or to put force or constraint on, or to intimidate or overawe the Government\"]"]
fields.penalty: On conviction, shall be sentenced to death.

fields.title: Trespass in place of worship, sepulture or funeral depository
fields.description: A person who, with intent to wound the feelings of any person or to insult the religion of any person, or with knowledge that such feelings or religion are likely to be insulted, commits any trespass in a place of worship, place of sepulture, place set apart for performance of funeral rites, or a depository for the remains of the dead.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused committed a trespass (unlawful entry or intrusion)\", \"The trespass occurred in one of the following places: a place of worship; a place of sepulture; a place set apart for the performance of funeral rites; or a depository for the remains of the dead\", \"The accused acted with either the intention of wounding the feelings of any person or of insulting the religion of any person, or with knowledge that the feelings of any person are likely to be wounded or that the religion of any person is likely to be insulted thereby\"]"]

fields.title: Trustees fraudulently disposing of trust property
fields.description: A person who, being a trustee of any property, and with intent to defraud, destroys the property or converts it to any use not authorised by the trust commits an offence. The offence is punishable by imprisonment for seven years.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a trustee of property\", \"The accused destroys the trust property or converts it to a use not authorised by the trust\", \"The accused acts with intent to defraud\"]"]

fields.title: Unauthorised administration of extra-judicial oath
fields.description: A person who administers an oath or makes a solemn declaration, affirmation or affidavit touching any matter with respect to which he has not, by law, any authority to do so commits an offence. The offence is punishable by a fine not exceeding five million shillings or imprisonment for one year.
fields.elements: ["[\"The accused is a person who administers an oath or makes a solemn declaration, affirmation or affidavit\", \"The oath, declaration, affirmation or affidavit touches a matter in respect of which the accused has no legal authority to administer or take such oath/declaration/affirmation/affidavit\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine not exceeding five million shillings or imprisonment for one year.

fields.title: Unauthorised making, mending, possession or disposal of dies, plates or instruments for making imitation postage stamps
fields.description: A person who, without lawful authority or excuse (the proof of which lies on him), makes or mends, or begins or prepares to make or mend, or uses, or has in his possession or disposes of any die, plate, instrument or material for making any imitation or representation of a postage stamp, commits an offence. The offender is liable to a fine of one hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for one year, and any such dies, plates, instruments or materials found in his possession shall be forfeited to the United Republic.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Acts without lawful authority or excuse (proof lies on the accused)\", \"Makes or mends, or begins or prepares to make or mend, or uses, or has in his possession or disposes of\", \"Any die, plate, instrument or material for making an imitation or representation of a stamp used for denoting any rate of postage of Tanzania or of any foreign country\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine of one hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for one year.

fields.title: Unauthorised making, use, possession or disposal of imitation postage stamps
fields.description: A person who, without lawful authority or excuse (the proof of which lies on him), makes or begins or prepares to make, uses for any postal purposes, has in his possession, or disposes of any imitation or representation on paper or any other material of any stamp used for denoting any rate of postage of Tanzania or of any foreign country, commits an offence. The offender is liable to a fine of one hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment for one year, and any stamps or other things referred to in this section found in his possession shall be forfeited to the United Republic.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Acts without lawful authority or excuse (proof lies on the accused)\", \"Makes, begins or prepares to make, or uses for any postal purposes, or has in his possession, or disposes of\", \"An imitation or representation on paper or any other material of any stamp used for denoting any rate of postage of Tanzania or of any foreign country\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine of one hundred thousand shillings or imprisonment for one year.

fields.title: Unauthorised signing of official registers or records
fields.description: A person who represents himself to be authorised by law to sign a document testifying to the contents of any register or record kept by lawful authority, or testifying to any fact or event, and signs such a document when he is not so authorised and knows that he is not, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"person represents himself to be authorised by law to sign a document testifying to the contents of a register or record kept by lawful authority or testifying to any fact or event\", \"signs the document as being so authorised\", \"is not in fact authorised to do so\"]"]

fields.title: Unlawful compounding of offences
fields.description: A person who asks for, receives, obtains, agrees to receive, or attempts to receive or obtain any property or benefit for themselves or another pursuant to any agreement or understanding that they will compound or conceal an offence, abstain from, discontinue, or delay a prosecution for an offence, or withhold any evidence of an offence, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person asks for, receives, obtains, agrees to receive, or attempts to receive or obtain property or any benefit\", \"The property or benefit is for the person or for any other person\", \"There is an agreement or understanding that the person will (a) compound or conceal an offence, or (b) abstain from, discontinue, or delay a prosecution for an offence, or (c) withhold any evidence of an offence\"]"]

fields.title: Unlawful compulsory labour
fields.description: A person who unlawfully compels any other person to labour against that person's will commits an offence. The provision criminalises forcing another to perform labour without their consent.
fields.elements: ["[\"Accused is a person who acts to compel\", \"There is another person who is compelled (victim)\", \"The accused compels the victim to labour\", \"The labour is against the victim's will\", \"The compulsion is unlawful\"]"]

fields.title: Unlawfully and maliciously causing bodily harm to an apprentice or servant endangering life or causing permanent injury
fields.description: A person who unlawfully and maliciously does or causes to be done any bodily harm to an apprentice or servant so that the life of that apprentice or servant is endangered or that his health has been or is likely to be permanently injured commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person does or causes to be done bodily harm to an apprentice or servant\", \"The conduct is unlawful and malicious\", \"The bodily harm so caused endangers the life of the apprentice/servant or has been or is likely to be permanently injurious to their health\"]"]

fields.title: Unlawfully hindering burial of a dead body
fields.description: A person who unlawfully hinders the burial of the dead body of any person commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Conduct: hinders (obstructs or prevents) the burial\", \"Object: the dead body of any person\", \"Unlawfulness (absence of lawful authority) in hindering the burial\"]"]

fields.title: Unlawful oaths to commit offences
fields.description: Creates an offence where a person either (a) administers, or is present at and consents to the administering of, an oath or engagement in the nature of an oath purporting to bind the taker to commit any offence punishable with death; or (b) takes any such oath or engagement, not being compelled to do so. On conviction the offender is liable to imprisonment for life.
fields.elements: ["[\"There is an oath or engagement in the nature of an oath that purports to bind the person who takes it to commit an offence punishable with death\", \"The accused either (i) administers the oath or is present and consents to its administration, or (ii) takes the oath (and is not compelled to do so)\"]"]

fields.title: Unlawful possession or conveying of Defence Forces stores
fields.description: A person conveying, having in his possession, or keeping in any building or place any stores that are the property of the Defence Forces of the United Republic which may reasonably be suspected of having been stolen or unlawfully obtained, and who fails to give an account to the satisfaction of the court of how he obtained it, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person conveys, has in possession, or keeps in any building or place any stores that are the property of the Defence Forces of the United Republic\", \"The stores may reasonably be suspected of having been stolen or unlawfully obtained\", \"The person fails to give an account to the satisfaction of the court of how he obtained the stores\"]"]

fields.title: Unlawful possession or conveying of public stores (Government property)
fields.description: A person charged with conveying, having in his possession, or keeping in any building or place any stores marked in accordance with subsection (1), which may be reasonably suspected of having been stolen or unlawfully obtained, and who fails to give an account to the satisfaction of the court of how he obtained it, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person conveys, has in possession, or keeps in any building or place any stores marked in accordance with subsection (1)\", \"The stores may be reasonably suspected of having been stolen or unlawfully obtained\", \"The person, when charged, fails to give an account to the satisfaction of the court of how he obtained the stores\"]"]

fields.title: Unlawful training or drilling of persons to the use of arms
fields.description: A person who, without the permission of the Minister responsible for home affairs, trains or drills any other person to the use of arms or the practice of military exercises, movements or evolutions commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor: a person\", \"Act: trains or drills any other person\", \"Subject matter of training: the use of arms or the practice of military exercises, movements or evolutions\", \"Condition: without the permission of the Minister responsible for home affairs\"]"]

fields.title: Unlawful wearing of uniform or attire in a manner likely to bring contempt, or causing another to do so
fields.description: A person who unlawfully wears the uniform or attire of the Defence Forces, police or law enforcement organs, or dress bearing their distinctive marks, in such a manner or circumstances as to be likely to bring contempt on that uniform, or who employs any other person to so wear the uniform or dress, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"A person\", \"Unlawfully wears the uniform or attire of the Defence Forces, police or law enforcement organs, or any dress having the appearance or bearing any of the regimental or other distinctive marks of the uniform\", \"Worn in such a manner or in such circumstances as to be likely to bring contempt on that uniform\", \"Or employs any other person to so wear the uniform or dress\"]"]
fields.penalty: A fine of four hundred shillings or imprisonment for three months.

fields.title: Unnatural offences
fields.description: A person who (a) has carnal knowledge of any person against the order of nature; (b) has carnal knowledge of an animal; or (c) permits a male person to have carnal knowledge of him or her against the order of nature, commits an offence and is liable to imprisonment for life and in any case to imprisonment for a term of not less than thirty years.
fields.elements: ["[\"Actor is a person who engages in the prohibited conduct\", \"Carnal knowledge of any person against the order of nature\", \"Carnal knowledge of an animal\", \"Permitting a male person to have carnal knowledge of the actor against the order of nature\"]"]
fields.penalty: Imprisonment for life and in any case to imprisonment for a term of not less than thirty years.

fields.title: Use of a previously used stamp knowing it was used, fraudulently or intending to cause loss to the public revenue
fields.description: A person who fraudulently, or with intent to cause loss to the public revenue, uses for any purpose any stamp which he knows to have been previously used, commits an offence.
fields.elements: ["[\"Person uses a stamp for any purpose\", \"Person knows the stamp has been previously used\", \"Use is fraudulent or done with intent to cause loss to the public revenue\"]"]

fields.title: Uttering cancelled or exhausted documents
